verses.ar: أمّا أنا فأقولُ لَكُم: إنَّ كُلَّ مَن يَغضَبُ على أخيهِ بِلا سَبَبٍ يَكونُ مُستَوجِباً لِلحُكم؛ ومَن قالَ لأخيهِ «رِيكا» يَكونُ مُستَوجِباً لِمَجلِسِ الجَماعة؛ ومَن قالَ «يا أحمق» يَكونُ مُستَوجِباً لِجَهَنَّمِ النار.

verses.syr: ܐܢ ܗܘ ܗܟܝܠ ܕܡܩܪܒ ܐܢܬ ܩܘܪܒܢܟ ܥܠ ܡܕܒܚܐ ܘܬܡܢ ܬܬܕܟܪ ܕܐܚܝܕ ܥܠܝܟ ܐܚܘܟ ܐܟܬܐ ܡܕܡ
verses.el: ἐὰν οὖν προσφέρῃς τὸ δῶρόν σου ἐπὶ τὸ θυσιαστήριον κἀκεῖ μνησθῇς ὅτι ὁ ἀδελφός σου ἔχει τι κατὰ σοῦ,
verses.en: If therefore you are offering your offering upon the altar, and there you remember that your brother holds something against you,
verses.ar: فإن كُنتَ تُقَدِّمُ قُربانَكَ على المَذبَح، وهُناكَ تَذَكَّرتَ أنَّ لأخيكَ شيئاً عَليك،

verses.syr: ܫܒܘܩ ܬܡܢ ܩܘܪܒܢܟ ܩܕܡ ܡܕܒܚܐ ܘܙܠ ܠܘܩܕܡ ܐܬܪܥܐ ܥܡ ܐܚܘܟ ܘܗܝܕܝܢ ܬܐ ܩܪܒ ܩܘܪܒܢܟ
verses.el: ἄφες ἐκεῖ τὸ δῶρόν σου ἔμπροσθεν τοῦ θυσιαστηρίου καὶ ὕπαγε πρῶτον διαλλάγηθι τῷ ἀδελφῷ σου, καὶ τότε ἐλθὼν πρόσφερε τὸ δῶρόν σου.
verses.en: leave your offering there before the altar and go first — be reconciled with your brother, and then come and offer your offering.
verses.ar: اترُك قُربانَكَ هُناكَ أمامَ المَذبَح واذهَب أوَّلاً — اصطَلِح مَعَ أخيك، ثُمَّ ائتِ وقَدِّم قُربانَك.

verses.syr: ܗܘܝܬ ܡܬܐܘܐ ܥܡ ܒܥܠ ܕܝܢܟ ܥܓܠ ܥܕ ܥܡܗ ܐܢܬ ܒܐܘܪܚܐ ܕܠܡܐ ܒܥܠ ܕܝܢܟ ܢܫܠܡܟ ܠܕܝܢܐ ܘܕܝܢܐ ܢܫܠܡܟ ܠܓܒܝܐ ܘܬܦܠ ܒܝܬ ܐܣܝܪܐ
verses.el: ἴσθι εὐνοῶν τῷ ἀντιδίκῳ σου ταχὺ ἕως ὅτου εἶ μετ’ αὐτοῦ ἐν τῇ ὁδῷ· μή ποτέ σε παραδῷ ὁ ἀντίδικος τῷ κριτῇ, καὶ ὁ κριτὴς τῷ ὑπηρέτῃ, καὶ εἰς φυλακὴν βληθήσῃ·
verses.en: Be in agreement with your adversary quickly, while you are with him on the way, lest your adversary deliver you to the judge, and the judge deliver you to the guard, and you fall into the prison-house.
verses.ar: كُن مُسالِماً لِخَصمِكَ سَريعاً وأنتَ مَعَهُ في الطَّريق، لِئَلّا يُسَلِّمَكَ الخَصمُ إلى القاضي، ويُسَلِّمَكَ القاضي إلى الحارِس، وتَقَعَ في السِّجن.

verses.syr: ܘܐܡܝܢ ܐܡܪ ܐܢܐ ܠܟ ܕܠܐ ܬܦܘܩ ܡܢ ܬܡܢ ܥܕܡܐ ܕܬܬܠ ܫܡܘܢܐ ܐܚܪܝܐ
verses.el: ἀμὴν λέγω σοι, οὐ μὴ ἐξέλθῃς ἐκεῖθεν ἕως ἂν ἀποδῷς τὸν ἔσχατον κοδράντην.
verses.en: And amen I say to you: you shall not come out from there until you have given the last coin.
verses.ar: الحَقَّ أقولُ لَك: لَن تَخرُجَ مِن هُناكَ حتّى تُوَفِّيَ آخِرَ فَلس.

verses.syr: ܫܡܥܬܘܢ ܕܐܬܐܡܪ ܕܠܐ ܬܓܘܪ
verses.el: Ἠκούσατε ὅτι ἐρρέθη Οὐ μοιχεύσεις.
verses.en: You have heard that it was said: "You shall not commit adultery."
verses.ar: سَمِعتُم أنَّهُ قِيل: «لا تَزنِ.»

verses.syr: ܐܢܐ ܕܝܢ ܐܡܪ ܐܢܐ ܠܟܘܢ ܕܟܠ ܡܢ ܕܚܙܐ ܐܢܬܬܐ ܐܝܟ ܕܢܪܓܝܗ ܡܚܕܐ ܓܪܗ ܒܠܒܗ
verses.el: ἐγὼ δὲ λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι πᾶς ὁ βλέπων γυναῖκα πρὸς τὸ ἐπιθυμῆσαι αὐτὴν ἤδη ἐμοίχευσεν αὐτὴν ἐν τῇ καρδίᾳ αὐτοῦ.
verses.en: But I say to you that every one who looks at a woman so as to desire her has already committed adultery with her in his heart.
verses.ar: أمّا أنا فأقولُ لَكُم: إنَّ كُلَّ مَن نَظَرَ إلى امرأةٍ بِشَهوةٍ، فقَد زَنى بِها في قَلبِهِ.

verses.syr: ܐܢ ܕܝܢ ܥܝܢܟ ܕܝܡܝܢܐ ܡܟܫܠܐ ܠܟ ܚܨܝܗ ܘܫܕܝܗ ܡܢܟ ܦܩܚ ܠܟ ܓܝܪ ܕܢܐܒܕ ܚܕ ܗܕܡܟ ܘܠܐ ܟܠܗ ܦܓܪܟ ܢܦܠ ܒܓܗܢܐ
verses.el: εἰ δὲ ὁ ὀφθαλμός σου ὁ δεξιὸς σκανδαλίζει σε, ἔξελε αὐτὸν καὶ βάλε ἀπὸ σοῦ· συμφέρει γάρ σοι ἵνα ἀπόληται ἓν τῶν μελῶν σου καὶ μὴ ὅλον τὸ σῶμά σου βληθῇ εἰς γέενναν.
verses.en: And if your right eye causes you to stumble, pluck it out and cast it from you; for it is better for you that one of your members perish than that your whole body fall into Gehenna.
verses.ar: فإن كانَت عَينُكَ اليُمنى تُعثِرُك، فاقلَعها وارمِ بِها عَنك؛ لأنَّهُ خَيرٌ لَكَ أن يَهلِكَ أحَدُ أعضائِكَ ولا يُلقى جَسَدُكَ كُلُّهُ في جَهَنَّم.

verses.syr: ܘܐܢ ܐܝܕܟ ܕܝܡܝܢܐ ܡܟܫܠܐ ܠܟ ܦܣܘܩ ܫܕܝܗ ܡܢܟ ܦܩܚ ܠܟ ܓܝܪ ܕܢܐܒܕ ܚܕ ܡܢ ܗܕܡܝܟ ܘܠܐ ܟܠܗ ܦܓܪܟ ܢܦܠ ܒܓܗܢܐ
verses.el: καὶ εἰ ἡ δεξιά σου χεὶρ σκανδαλίζει σε, ἔκκοψον αὐτὴν καὶ βάλε ἀπὸ σοῦ· συμφέρει γάρ σοι ἵνα ἀπόληται ἓν τῶν μελῶν σου καὶ μὴ ὅλον τὸ σῶμά σου εἰς γέενναν ἀπέλθῃ.
verses.en: And if your right hand causes you to stumble, cut it off and cast it from you; for it is better for you that one of your members perish than that your whole body fall into Gehenna.
verses.ar: وإن كانَت يَدُكَ اليُمنى تُعثِرُك، فاقطَعها وارمِ بِها عَنك؛ لأنَّهُ خَيرٌ لَكَ أن يَهلِكَ أحَدُ أعضائِكَ ولا يُلقى جَسَدُكَ كُلُّهُ في جَهَنَّم.

verses.syr: ܐܬܐܡܪ ܕܡܢ ܕܫܪܐ ܐܢܬܬܗ ܢܬܠ ܠܗ ܟܬܒܐ ܕܕܘܠܠܐ
verses.el: Ἐρρέθη δέ Ὃς ἂν ἀπολύσῃ τὴν γυναῖκα αὐτοῦ, δότω αὐτῇ ἀποστάσιον.
verses.en: It was said: "Whoever releases his wife, let him give her a writing of release."
verses.ar: قِيلَ: «مَن أطلَقَ امرأتَهُ فَليُعطِها كِتابَ طَلاق.»

verses.syr: ܐܢܐ ܕܝܢ ܐܡܪ ܐܢܐ ܠܟܘܢ ܕܟܠ ܡܢ ܕܫܪܐ ܐܢܬܬܗ ܠܒܪ ܡܢ ܡܠܬܐ ܕܙܢܝܘܬܐ ܥܒܕ ܠܗ ܕܬܓܘܪ ܘܡܢ ܕܫܩܠ ܫܒܝܩܬܐ ܓܐܪ
verses.el: ἐγὼ δὲ λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι πᾶς ὁ ἀπολύων τὴν γυναῖκα αὐτοῦ παρεκτὸς λόγου πορνείας ποιεῖ αὐτὴν μοιχευθῆναι, καὶ ὃς ἐὰν ἀπολελυμένην γαμήσῃ μοιχᾶται.
verses.en: But I say to you that whoever releases his wife, apart from a matter of fornication, causes her to commit adultery; and whoever takes a released woman commits adultery.
verses.ar: أمّا أنا فأقولُ لَكُم: إنَّ كُلَّ مَن أطلَقَ امرأتَهُ، إلّا مِن أجلِ الزِّنا، يَجعَلُها تَزني؛ ومَن تَزَوَّجَ مُطَلَّقةً فقَد زَنى.

verses.syr: ܬܘܒ ܫܡܥܬܘܢ ܕܐܬܐܡܪ ܠܩܕܡܝܐ ܕܠܐ ܬܕܓܠ ܒܡܘܡܬܟ ܬܫܠܡ ܕܝܢ ܠܡܪܝܐ ܡܘܡܬܟ
verses.el: Πάλιν ἠκούσατε ὅτι ἐρρέθη τοῖς ἀρχαίοις Οὐκ ἐπιορκήσεις, ἀποδώσεις δὲ τῷ Κυρίῳ τοὺς ὅρκους σου.
verses.en: Again, you have heard that it was said to the ancients: "You shall not swear falsely in your oaths, but you shall fulfill your oaths to the Lord."
verses.ar: أيضاً سَمِعتُم أنَّهُ قِيلَ لِلقُدَماء: «لا تَحنَث في أيمانِك، بل أوفِ لِلرَّبِّ أيمانَك.»

verses.syr: ܐܢܐ ܕܝܢ ܐܡܪ ܐܢܐ ܠܟܘܢ ܠܐ ܬܐܡܘܢ ܣܟ ܠܐ ܒܫܡܝܐ ܕܟܘܪܣܝܐ ܗܘ ܕܐܠܗܐ
verses.el: ἐγὼ δὲ λέγω ὑμῖν μὴ ὀμόσαι ὅλως· μήτε ἐν τῷ οὐρανῷ, ὅτι θρόνος ἐστὶν τοῦ Θεοῦ·
verses.en: But I say to you: do not swear at all — not by the heavens, for it is the throne of God;
verses.ar: أمّا أنا فأقولُ لَكُم: لا تَحلِفوا البَتَّةَ — لا بِالسَّماء، فإنَّها عَرشُ الله؛

verses.syr: ܘܠܐ ܒܐܪܥܐ ܕܟܘܒܫܐ ܗܝ ܕܬܚܝܬ ܪܓܠܘܗܝ ܐܦܠܐ ܒܐܘܪܝܫܠܡ ܕܡܕܝܢܬܗ ܗܝ ܕܡܠܟܐ ܪܒܐ
verses.el: μήτε ἐν τῇ γῇ, ὅτι ὑποπόδιόν ἐστιν τῶν ποδῶν αὐτοῦ· μήτε εἰς Ἱεροσόλυμα, ὅτι πόλις ἐστὶν τοῦ μεγάλου Βασιλέως·
verses.en: nor by the earth, for it is the footstool beneath His feet; nor by Yerushalem, for it is the city of the great Sovereign.
verses.ar: ولا بِالأرض، فإنَّها مَوطِئُ قَدَمَيه؛ ولا بِأورشليم، فإنَّها مَدينةُ المَلِكِ العَظيم.

verses.syr: ܐܦܠܐ ܒܪܫܟ ܬܐܡܐ ܕܠܐ ܡܫܟܚ ܐܢܬ ܠܡܥܒܕ ܒܗ ܡܢܬܐ ܚܕܐ ܕܣܥܪܐ ܐܘܟܡܬܐ ܐܘ ܚܘܪܬܐ
verses.el: μήτε ἐν τῇ κεφαλῇ σου ὀμόσῃς, ὅτι οὐ δύνασαι μίαν τρίχα λευκὴν ποιῆσαι ἢ μέλαιναν.
verses.en: Nor shall you swear by your head, for you cannot make one hair of it black or white.
verses.ar: ولا تَحلِف بِرَأسِكَ، لأنَّكَ لا تَقدِرُ أن تَجعَلَ شَعرةً واحِدةً مِنهُ بَيضاءَ أو سَوداء.

verses.syr: ܐܠܐ ܬܗܘܐ ܡܠܬܟܘܢ ܐܝܢ ܐܝܢ ܘܠܐ ܠܐ ܡܕܡ ܕܡܢ ܗܠܝܢ ܝܬܝܪ ܡܢ ܒܝܫܐ ܗܘ
verses.el: ἔστω δὲ ὁ λόγος ὑμῶν ναὶ ναί, οὒ οὔ· τὸ δὲ περισσὸν τούτων ἐκ τοῦ πονηροῦ ἐστιν.
verses.en: But let your word be: Yes, yes, and No, no. Whatever is more than these is from the evil one.
verses.ar: بل لِيَكُن كَلامُكُم: نَعَم نَعَم، ولا لا. وما زادَ على ذلك فهو مِن الشَّرير.

verses.syr: ܫܡܥܬܘܢ ܕܐܬܐܡܪ ܕܥܝܢܐ ܚܠܦ ܥܝܢܐ ܘܫܢܐ ܚܠܦ ܫܢܐ
verses.el: Ἠκούσατε ὅτι ἐρρέθη Ὀφθαλμὸν ἀντὶ ὀφθαλμοῦ καὶ ὀδόντα ἀντὶ ὀδόντος.
verses.en: You have heard that it was said: "An eye for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth."
verses.ar: سَمِعتُم أنَّهُ قِيل: «عَيناً بِعَين وسِنًّا بِسِنّ.»

verses.syr: ܐܢܐ ܕܝܢ ܐܡܪ ܐܢܐ ܠܟܘܢ ܕܠܐ ܬܩܘܡܘܢ ܠܘܩܒܠ ܒܝܫܐ ܐܠܐ ܡܢ ܕܡܚܐ ܠܟ ܥܠ ܦܟܟ ܕܝܡܝܢܐ ܐܦܢܐ ܠܗ ܐܦ ܐܚܪܢܐ
verses.el: ἐγὼ δὲ λέγω ὑμῖν μὴ ἀντιστῆναι τῷ πονηρῷ· ἀλλ’ ὅστις σε ῥαπίζει εἰς τὴν δεξιὰν σιαγόνα σου, στρέψον αὐτῷ καὶ τὴν ἄλλην·
verses.en: But I say to you: do not stand against the evil one. Rather, whoever strikes you on your right cheek, turn to him the other also.
verses.ar: أمّا أنا فأقولُ لَكُم: لا تُقاوِموا الشَّرير. بل مَن ضَرَبَكَ على خَدِّكَ الأيمَن، فأدِر له الآخَرَ أيضاً.

verses.syr: ܘܡܢ ܕܨܒܐ ܕܢܕܘܢ ܥܡܟ ܘܢܫܩܘܠ ܟܘܬܝܢܟ ܫܒܘܩ ܠܗ ܐܦ ܡܪܛܘܛܟ
verses.el: καὶ τῷ θέλοντί σοι κριθῆναι καὶ τὸν χιτῶνά σου λαβεῖν, ἄφες αὐτῷ καὶ τὸ ἱμάτιον·
verses.en: And whoever wishes to bring judgment against you and take your tunic, leave him also your cloak.
verses.ar: ومَن أرادَ أن يُحاكِمَكَ ويَأخُذَ ثَوبَك، فاترُك له الرِّداءَ أيضاً.

verses.syr: ܡܢ ܕܡܫܚܪ ܠܟ ܡܝܠܐ ܚܕ ܙܠ ܥܡܗ ܬܪܝܢ
verses.el: καὶ ὅστις σε ἀγγαρεύσει μίλιον ἕν, ὕπαγε μετ’ αὐτοῦ δύο.
verses.en: Whoever compels you one mile, go with him two.
verses.ar: ومَن سَخَّرَكَ مِيلاً واحِداً، فاذهَب مَعَهُ اثنَين.

verses.syr: ܡܢ ܕܫܐܠ ܠܟ ܗܒ ܠܗ ܘܡܢ ܕܨܒܐ ܕܢܐܙܦ ܡܢܟ ܠܐ ܬܟܠܝܘܗܝ
verses.el: τῷ αἰτοῦντί σε δός, καὶ τὸν θέλοντα ἀπὸ σοῦ δανίσασθαι μὴ ἀποστραφῇς.
verses.en: Whoever asks of you — give to him; and whoever wishes to borrow from you — do not refuse him.
verses.ar: مَن سَأَلَكَ فأعطِهِ؛ ومَن أرادَ أن يَستَقرِضَ مِنكَ فلا تَرُدَّه.

verses.syr: ܫܡܥܬܘܢ ܕܐܬܐܡܪ ܕܪܚܡ ܠܩܪܝܒܟ ܘܣܢܝ ܠܒܥܠܕܒܒܟ
verses.el: Ἠκούσατε ὅτι ἐρρέθη Ἀγαπήσεις τὸν πλησίον σου καὶ μισήσεις τὸν ἐχθρόν σου.
verses.en: You have heard that it was said: "Love your neighbor, and hate your enemy."
verses.ar: سَمِعتُم أنَّهُ قِيل: «أحبِب قَريبَكَ وأبغِض عَدُوَّك.»

verses.syr: ܐܢܐ ܕܝܢ ܐܡܪ ܐܢܐ ܠܟܘܢ ܐܚܒܘ ܠܒܥܠܕܒܒܝܟܘܢ ܘܒܪܟܘ ܠܡܢ ܕܠܐܛ ܠܟܘܢ ܘܥܒܕܘ ܕܫܦܝܪ ܠܡܢ ܕܣܢܐ ܠܟܘܢ ܘܨܠܘ ܥܠ ܐܝܠܝܢ ܕܕܒܪܝܢ ܠܟܘܢ ܒܩܛܝܪܐ ܘܪܕܦܝܢ ܠܟܘܢ
verses.el: ἐγὼ δὲ λέγω ὑμῖν, ἀγαπᾶτε τοὺς ἐχθροὺς ὑμῶν καὶ προσεύχεσθε ὑπὲρ τῶν διωκόντων ὑμᾶς·
verses.en: But I say to you: love your enemies, and bless those who curse you, and do what is good to those who hate you, and pray for those who seize you by force and pursue you —
verses.ar: أمّا أنا فأقولُ لَكُم: أحِبُّوا أعداءَكُم، وبارِكوا لاعِنيكُم، وأحسِنوا إلى مُبغِضيكُم، وصَلُّوا مِن أجلِ الذينَ يَعتَدونَ عَليكُم ويَطرُدونَكُم —

verses.syr: ܐܝܟܢܐ ܕܬܗܘܘܢ ܒܢܘܗܝ ܕܐܒܘܟܘܢ ܕܒܫܡܝܐ ܗܘ ܕܡܕܢܚ ܫܡܫܗ ܥܠ ܛܒܐ ܘܥܠ ܒܝܫܐ ܘܡܚܬ ܡܛܪܗ ܥܠ ܟܐܢܐ ܘܥܠ ܥܘܠܐ
verses.el: ὅπως γένησθε υἱοὶ τοῦ Πατρὸς ὑμῶν τοῦ ἐν οὐρανοῖς, ὅτι τὸν ἥλιον αὐτοῦ ἀνατέλλει ἐπὶ πονηροὺς καὶ ἀγαθοὺς καὶ βρέχει ἐπὶ δικαίους καὶ ἀδίκους.
verses.en: so that you may be children of your Father who is in the heavens, who makes His sun rise upon the good and upon the evil, and sends His rain upon the right and upon the wrong.
verses.ar: لِتَكونوا أبناءَ أبيكُمُ الذي في السَّماوات، الذي يُطلِعُ شَمسَهُ على الأخيارِ والأشرار، ويُرسِلُ مَطَرَهُ على الأبرارِ والظّالِمين.

verses.syr: ܐܢ ܓܝܪ ܡܚܒܝܢ ܐܢܬܘܢ ܠܐܝܠܝܢ ܕܡܚܒܝܢ ܠܟܘܢ ܡܢܐ ܐܓܪܐ ܐܝܬ ܠܟܘܢ ܠܐ ܗܐ ܐܦ ܡܟܣܐ ܗܝ ܗܕܐ ܥܒܕܝܢ
verses.el: ἐὰν γὰρ ἀγαπήσητε τοὺς ἀγαπῶντας ὑμᾶς, τίνα μισθὸν ἔχετε; οὐχὶ καὶ οἱ τελῶναι τὸ αὐτὸ ποιοῦσιν;
verses.en: For if you love those who love you, what reward have you? Do not even the tax-collectors do the same?
verses.ar: فإن أحبَبتُم الذينَ يُحِبُّونَكُم، فأيُّ أجرٍ لَكُم؟ أوَلا يَفعَلُ ذلكَ العَشّارونَ أيضاً؟

verses.syr: ܘܐܢ ܫܐܠܝܢ ܐܢܬܘܢ ܒܫܠܡܐ ܕܐܚܝܟܘܢ ܒܠܚܘܕ ܡܢܐ ܝܬܝܪ ܥܒܕܝܢ ܐܢܬܘܢ ܠܐ ܗܐ ܐܦ ܡܟܣܐ ܗܝ ܗܕܐ ܥܒܕܝܢ
verses.el: καὶ ἐὰν ἀσπάσησθε τοὺς ἀδελφοὺς ὑμῶν μόνον, τί περισσὸν ποιεῖτε; οὐχὶ καὶ οἱ ἐθνικοὶ τὸ αὐτὸ ποιοῦσιν;
verses.en: And if you greet your brothers only, what more do you do? Do not even the tax-collectors do the same?
verses.ar: وإن سَلَّمتُم على إخوَتِكُم فقط، فماذا تَزيدون؟ أوَلا يَفعَلُ ذلكَ العَشّارونَ أيضاً؟

verses.syr: ܗܘܘ ܗܟܝܠ ܐܢܬܘܢ ܓܡܝܪܐ ܐܝܟܢܐ ܕܐܒܘܟܘܢ ܕܒܫܡܝܐ ܓܡܝܪ ܗܘ
verses.el: Ἔσεσθε οὖν ὑμεῖς τέλειοι ὡς ὁ Πατὴρ ὑμῶν ὁ οὐράνιος τέλειός ἐστιν.
verses.en: Be therefore whole, even as your Father who is in the heavens is whole.
verses.ar: فكونوا كامِلينَ كما أنَّ أباكُمُ الذي في السَّماواتِ كامِل.

chapters.commentary:
# Annotations on Matthew Chapter 5 — The Teaching on the Mountain

## Syriac-First Approach

This chapter is translated primarily from the Syriac Peshitta, with Greek noted where the two diverge. The Peshitta preserves Aramaic — the language Jesus actually spoke. The Greek New Testament is already a translation; the Syriac may be closer to the original words.

## Root-Recovery Choices

### v.3-11: ܛܘܒܝܗܘܢ (ṭuvayhun) — "Blessed are..."
Root ܛ-ܘ-ܒ (ṭ-w-b): good, well, blessed, flourishing. This is NOT the beatitude formula "blessed" in the sense of liturgical pronouncement. ܛܘܒܐ (ṭuva) means "goodness, well-being, flourishing" — it is an exclamation of congratulation or felicitation: "How flourishing are..." or "What well-being belongs to...!" Cognate with Hebrew טוב (tov, "good") — as in God seeing that creation was "good/ṭov." Arabic طيب (ṭayyib, "good/pure/pleasant"). The conventional "Blessed are" sounds passive and religious; the Aramaic exclaims the thriving reality of those who enter the sovereignty's way.

### v.3: ܡܣܟܢܐ ܒܪܘܚ (maskena b-ruḥ) — "poor in breath"
Root ܡ-ܣ-ܟ-ܢ (m-s-k-n): poor, needy. ܒܪܘܚ (b-ruḥ): in breath/spirit. The Greek πτωχοὶ τῷ πνεύματι (ptōchoi tō pneumati) is parallel. The poverty here is poverty IN BREATH — in spirit, in self-assertion, in the vital energy of ego. The image is of those who hold themselves empty before God, making no claims from the fullness of their own ܪܘܚܐ (breath). This resonates with the Arabic فقر (faqr, "poverty/neediness before God") as a Sufi virtue — the emptying that allows divine fullness.

### v.3: ܡܠܟܘܬܐ ܕܫܡܝܐ (malkuta d-shmaya) — "sovereignty of the heavens"
The key phrase of the Beatitudes and the entire Sermon on the Mount. This sovereignty does NOT belong to the powerful — it belongs to the poor in breath, those who mourn, the lowly. This inverts every human power calculation. See detailed treatment in ch. 6 notes.

### v.5: ܡܟܝܟܐ (makikhe) — "the lowly"
Root ܡ-ܟ-ܟ (m-k-k) / ܟ-ܘ-ܟ (k-w-k): low, humble, brought down. The conventional "meek" has acquired connotations of weakness or passivity. The Syriac ܡܟܝܟܐ means genuinely lowly — those who do not exalt themselves. Cognate with Hebrew עניו (anav, "lowly/humble") as used in Psalm 37:11 — the very verse being quoted here in the Beatitude. Arabic تواضع (tawāḍuʿ, "humility/lowliness"). The ܡܟܝܟܐ shall inherit the earth — the same promise as Psalm 37.

### v.6: ܟܦܢܝܢ ܘܨܗܝܢ ܠܙܕܝܩܘܬܐ (kafniyn w-tsahyin l-zadiquta) — "hunger and thirst for rightness"
Root ܙ-ܕ-ܩ (z-d-q): rightness, justice. Not merely personal righteousness but the state of things being RIGHT — justice in the social sense. The Greek δικαιοσύνη (dikaiosynē) carries the same full range from personal integrity to social justice. Those who hunger for the world to be made right — for ṭsedeq/zadiquta — shall be satisfied. The physical intensity of hunger and thirst makes this a passionate longing, not a polite preference.

### v.7: ܡܪܚܡܢܐ (marḥamane) — "the womb-merciful"
Root ܪ-ܚ-ܡ (r-ḥ-m): womb, mercy, compassion. ܪܚܡܐ (raḥma) = mercy; ܡܪܚܡܢܐ = those who show mercy. This is the most important cross-scriptural root in the entire New Testament. The same root as Hebrew רחמים (raḥamim, "compassions/mercies"), Arabic رحمة (raḥma, "mercy") — and the most prominent divine names in the Qur'an: الرحمن الرحيم (al-Raḥmān al-Raḥīm, "the Womb-Compassionate, the Womb-Merciful"). The Beatitude of mercy is couched in the same root as the divine names that open every chapter of the Qur'an. Upon the mercy-showers, mercy shall come (passive divine — God as implied subject): the same mercy they embody returns to them.

### v.8: ܕܟܝܐ ܒܠܒܗܘܢ (dakye b-lebhun) — "pure in their heart"
Root ܕ-ܟ-ܝ (d-k-y): clean, pure, unmixed. ܠܒܐ (leba, "heart") — the center of will and intention. Cognate with Hebrew לב (lev, "heart") and Arabic لب (lubb, "heart/core"). Purity of heart is singleness of intention — the ܦܫܝܛܐ (pshiṭa, "simple/single") eye of ch. 6:22 applied to the entire person. Greek καθαροὶ τῇ καρδίᾳ (katharoi tē kardia) matches exactly.

### v.9: ܥܒܕܝ ܫܠܡܐ (ʿavday shlama) — "makers of peace"
Root ܫ-ܠ-ܡ (sh-l-m): peace, wholeness, completion, submission. The root of Hebrew שלום (shalom), Arabic سلام (salām) and Islam (إسلام, "submission/peace"). The "makers of peace" are those who actively bring about shalom/shlama — wholeness and reconciliation. They are called "children of God" (ܒܢܝ ܐܠܗܐ, bnay alaha). Greek εἰρηνοποιοί (eirēnopoioi) = "peace-makers."

### v.13: ܡܠܚܐ ܕܐܪܥܐ (malḥa d-arʿa) — "salt of the earth"
Root ܡ-ܠ-ḥ (m-l-ḥ): salt. Salt preserves, flavors, purifies. The disciples are not called to be separate from the earth but to be its salt — transforming it from within. If salt loses its saltiness (ܦܟܗ, pekeh, "becomes insipid/tasteless"), it can no longer function. The root-recovery translation preserves "earth" (ܐܪܥܐ, arʿa) rather than "world" — grounding the image physically.

### v.14: ܢܘܗܪܐ ܕܥܠܡܐ (nuhara d-ʿalama) — "light of the age"
Note: the translation renders ܥܠܡܐ (ʿalama) as "age" here, consistent with the root-recovery choice. "Light of the world" (conventional) makes it spatial; "light of the age" makes it temporal — the light for this era, this epoch. Cognate with Hebrew עולם (olam) and Arabic عالم (ʿālam).

### v.20: ܦܪܝܫܐ (Perishe) — "Separated-ones"
See ch. 3 notes. The rightness that exceeds the scribes and the Separated-ones is not more rule-following but a deeper orientation — the difference between external compliance and internal transformation.

### v.21-48: ܫܡܥܬܘܢ ... ܐܢܐ ܕܝܢ ܐܡܪ ܐܢܐ ܠܟܘܢ (shmaʿtun... ana den amar ana lekhun) — "You have heard... But I say to you"
This antithetical formula recurs six times. The Anointed does not abrogate the Torah but deepens it — moving from outward act to inner root. "You have heard 'do not murder'" → "I say: do not even harbor anger." The pattern of deepening rather than abolishing is central to Matthew's theology and resonates with the Bahá'í principle that each revelation clarifies and deepens what preceded it.

### v.22: ܓܗܢܐ ܕܢܘܪܐ (gehanna d-nura) — "Gehenna of fire"
ܓܗܢܐ (gehanna) is the valley of Hinnom (Hebrew גֵּי הִנֹּם, gey hinnom) — a ravine south of Jerusalem associated with burning refuse and historically with child sacrifice. It is a PLACE, not an abstract afterlife concept. The root-recovery translation retains "Gehenna" rather than "hell" — which imports entirely different mythological associations. The Gehenna of fire is the ultimate consequence of the path of fragmentation, hostility, and contempt.

### v.48: ܗܘܘ ܓܡܝܪܐ (haway gmire) — "Be whole"
Root ܓ-ܡ-ܪ (g-m-r): complete, whole, finished, accomplished. The Greek τέλειοι (teleioi) = "complete, perfect, mature." But the conventional "Be perfect" sets an impossible individual standard. "Be WHOLE" — as your Father who is in the heavens is whole — suggests an integrative completeness, a wholeness that encompasses everything rather than an error-free perfection. The same root as ܓܡܪ (gamar, "to complete/accomplish").

## Cross-Scriptural Root Connections

| Syriac | Hebrew | Arabic | Shared meaning | Qur'an connection |
|--------|--------|--------|---------------|-------------------|
| ܪܚܡܐ (raḥma) | רחמים (raḥamim) | رحمة (raḥma) | womb-mercy/compassion | 1:3 — الرحمن الرحيم |
| ܫܠܡܐ (shlama) | שלום (shalom) | سلام (salām) | peace/wholeness | 6:54 — سلام عليكم |
| ܛܘܒ (ṭuv) | טוב (tov) | طيب (ṭayyib) | goodness/flourishing | 2:168 — طيبا (pure/good) |
| ܡܠܟܘܬܐ (malkuta) | מלכות (malkhut) | ملك (mulk) | sovereignty | 1:4 — مالك يوم الدين |
| ܙܕܩ (zadq) | צדק (tsedeq) | صدق (sidq) | rightness/justice | Throughout |
| ܓܗܢ (gehan) | גיהנם (gehinnom) | جهنم (jahannam) | Gehenna/fire-place | 2:119 — أصحاب النار |

## Bahá'í Interpretive Notes

The Beatitudes open with the poor in breath and close with those who are pursued on account of rightness — a frame that encompasses all who travel the way of the sovereignty against the grain of conventional power. Bahá'u'lláh's Hidden Words contains numerous echoes: "Possess a pure, kindly and radiant heart" (cf. v.8); "Be generous in prosperity and thankful in adversity" (cf. the mourners in v.4). The command "Be whole as your Father is whole" (v.48) parallels the Bahá'í teaching that the human being is a mirror of divine attributes — the goal is not sinlessness but reflection of divine wholeness. The "makers of peace" (v.9) are called children of God, consonant with the Bahá'í understanding that spiritual kinship — not biological lineage — defines the family of God.

## Integrative Links

- **v.3-11 Beatitudes ↔ Psalm 1**: Both open with ܛܘܒ/אשרי (flourishing of the one who...) and contrast two ways. The Beatitudes extend and deepen the Psalm's teaching.
- **v.5 "inherit the earth" ↔ Psalm 37:11**: ועניים יירשו ארץ — "The lowly shall inherit the earth" — direct quotation. The Aramaic Beatitude cites the Hebrew psalm.
- **v.7 "womb-merciful" ↔ Qur'an 1:1,3**: The رحمن رحيم frame of the Fatihah — God is supremely ܡܪܚܡܢܐ, and those who embody this receive it in return.
- **v.9 "makers of peace" ↔ Qur'an 49:10**: إنما المؤمنون إخوة فأصلحوا بين أخويكم — "The believers are brothers — make peace between your brothers." The same call to active peace-making.
- **v.44 "love your enemies" ↔ Torah**: This is not against the Torah — Leviticus 19:18 says "love your neighbor," but Proverbs 25:21 already says "if your enemy is hungry, feed him." The Anointed draws out what is latent in the tradition.
- **v.48 "Be whole" ↔ Leviticus 19:2**: קדשים תהיו (qedoshim tihyu, "Be holy") — the parallel command in Torah. "Wholeness" interprets "holiness" as integrative completeness rather than ritual separation.

chapters.title: Matthew — ܩܦܠܐܘܢ 6
chapters.subtitle: Matthew — Chapter 6

verses.syr: ܚܘܪܘ ܕܝܢ ܒܙܕܩܬܟܘܢ ܕܠܐ ܬܥܒܕܘܢܗ ܩܕܡ ܒܢܝ ܐܢܫܐ ܐܝܟ ܕܬܬܚܙܘܢ ܠܗܘܢ ܘܐܠܐ ܐܓܪܐ ܠܝܬ ܠܟܘܢ ܠܘܬ ܐܒܘܟܘܢ ܕܒܫܡܝܐ
verses.el: Προσέχετε δὲ τὴν δικαιοσύνην ὑμῶν μὴ ποιεῖν ἔμπροσθεν τῶν ἀνθρώπων πρὸς τὸ θεαθῆναι αὐτοῖς· εἰ δὲ μήγε, μισθὸν οὐκ ἔχετε παρὰ τῷ Πατρὶ ὑμῶν τῷ ἐν τοῖς οὐρανοῖς.
verses.en: But take heed of your rightness-giving, that you do it not before the sons of men so as to be seen by them; otherwise you have no reward with your Father who is in the heavens.
verses.ar: إيّاكُم أن تَصنَعوا صَدَقَتَكُم أمامَ النّاسِ لِيَنظُروكُم؛ وإلّا فلا أجرَ لَكُم عِندَ أبيكُمُ الذي في السَّماوات.

verses.syr: ܐܡܬܝ ܗܟܝܠ ܕܥܒܕ ܐܢܬ ܙܕܩܬܐ ܠܐ ܬܩܪܐ ܩܪܢܐ ܩܕܡܝܟ ܐܝܟ ܕܥܒܕܝܢ ܢܣܒܝ ܒܐܦܐ ܒܟܢܘܫܬܐ ܘܒܫܘܩܐ ܐܝܟ ܕܢܫܬܒܚܘܢ ܡܢ ܒܢܝ ܐܢܫܐ ܘܐܡܝܢ ܐܡܪ ܐܢܐ ܠܟܘܢ ܕܩܒܠܘ ܐܓܪܗܘܢ
verses.el: Ὅταν οὖν ποιῇς ἐλεημοσύνην, μὴ σαλπίσῃς ἔμπροσθέν σου, ὥσπερ οἱ ὑποκριταὶ ποιοῦσιν ἐν ταῖς συναγωγαῖς καὶ ἐν ταῖς ῥύμαις, ὅπως δοξασθῶσιν ὑπὸ τῶν ἀνθρώπων· ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν, ἀπέχουσιν τὸν μισθὸν αὐτῶν.
verses.en: When therefore you do a rightness-deed, do not sound a horn before you, as the face-takers do in the assemblies and in the marketplaces, so that they may be praised by the sons of men. Amen I say to you: they have received their reward.
verses.ar: فمَتى صَنَعتَ صَدَقةً، لا تُنفِخ بُوقاً أمامَك كما يَصنَعُ آخِذو الوُجوهِ في الجَماعاتِ وفي الأسواق، لِيُمَجَّدوا مِن النّاس. الحَقَّ أقولُ لَكُم: قَد استَوفَوا أجرَهُم.

verses.syr: ܐܢܬ ܕܝܢ ܡܐ ܕܥܒܕ ܐܢܬ ܙܕܩܬܐ ܠܐ ܬܕܥ ܣܡܠܟ ܡܢܐ ܥܒܕܐ ܝܡܝܢܟ
verses.el: σοῦ δὲ ποιοῦντος ἐλεημοσύνην μὴ γνώτω ἡ ἀριστερά σου τί ποιεῖ ἡ δεξιά σου,
verses.en: But you, when you do a rightness-deed, let not your left hand know what your right hand does,
verses.ar: بل أنتَ مَتى صَنَعتَ صَدَقةً، فلا تَعلَم شِمالُكَ ما تَصنَعُ يَمينُك،

verses.syr: ܐܝܟ ܕܬܗܘܐ ܙܕܩܬܟ ܒܟܣܝܐ ܘܐܒܘܟ ܕܚܙܐ ܒܟܣܝܐ ܗܘ ܢܦܪܥܟ ܒܓܠܝܐ
verses.el: ὅπως ᾖ σου ἡ ἐλεημοσύνη ἐν τῷ κρυπτῷ· καὶ ὁ Πατήρ σου ὁ βλέπων ἐν τῷ κρυπτῷ ἀποδώσει σοι.
verses.en: so that your rightness-deed may be in the hidden; and your Father who sees in the hidden — He shall repay you in the open.
verses.ar: حتّى تَكونَ صَدَقَتُكَ في الخَفاء؛ وأبوكَ الذي يَرى في الخَفاءِ — هو يُجازيكَ في العَلَن.

verses.syr: ܘܡܐ ܕܡܨܠܐ ܐܢܬ ܠܐ ܬܗܘܐ ܐܝܟ ܢܣܒܝ ܒܐܦܐ ܕܪܚܡܝܢ ܠܡܩܡ ܒܟܢܘܫܬܐ ܘܒܙܘܝܬܐ ܕܫܘܩܐ ܠܡܨܠܝܘ ܕܢܬܚܙܘܢ ܠܒܢܝ ܐܢܫܐ ܘܐܡܝܢ ܐܡܪ ܐܢܐ ܠܟܘܢ ܕܩܒܠܘ ܐܓܪܗܘܢ
verses.el: Καὶ ὅταν προσεύχησθε, οὐκ ἔσεσθε ὡς οἱ ὑποκριταί· ὅτι φιλοῦσιν ἐν ταῖς συναγωγαῖς καὶ ἐν ταῖς γωνίαις τῶν πλατειῶν ἑστῶτες προσεύχεσθαι, ὅπως φανῶσιν τοῖς ἀνθρώποις· ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν, ἀπέχουσιν τὸν μισθὸν αὐτῶν.
verses.en: And when you pray, be not like the face-takers, who love to stand in the assemblies and at the corners of the marketplaces to pray, so that they may be seen by the sons of men. Amen I say to you: they have received their reward.
verses.ar: وإذا صَلَّيتَ فلا تَكُن كَآخِذي الوُجوه، الذينَ يُحِبُّونَ أن يُصَلُّوا قائِمينَ في الجَماعاتِ وفي زَوايا الأسواق، لِيَنظُرَهُمُ النّاس. الحَقَّ أقولُ لَكُم: قَد استَوفَوا أجرَهُم.

verses.syr: ܐܢܬ ܕܝܢ ܐܡܬܝ ܕܡܨܠܐ ܐܢܬ ܥܘܠ ܠܬܘܢܟ ܘܐܚܘܕ ܬܪܥܟ ܘܨܠܐ ܠܐܒܘܟ ܕܒܟܣܝܐ ܘܐܒܘܟ ܕܚܙܐ ܒܟܣܝܐ ܢܦܪܥܟ ܒܓܠܝܐ
verses.el: σὺ δὲ ὅταν προσεύχῃ, εἴσελθε εἰς τὸ ταμεῖόν σου καὶ κλείσας τὴν θύραν σου πρόσευξαι τῷ Πατρί σου τῷ ἐν τῷ κρυπτῷ· καὶ ὁ Πατήρ σου ὁ βλέπων ἐν τῷ κρυπτῷ ἀποδώσει σοι.
verses.en: But you, when you pray, enter into your inner room and shut your door, and pray to your Father who is in the hidden; and your Father who sees in the hidden shall repay you in the open.
verses.ar: بل أنتَ إذا صَلَّيتَ، فادخُل إلى مَخدَعِكَ وأغلِق بابَك، وصَلِّ لأبيكَ الذي في الخَفاء؛ وأبوكَ الذي يَرى في الخَفاءِ يُجازيكَ في العَلَن.

verses.syr: ܘܡܐ ܕܡܨܠܝܢ ܐܢܬܘܢ ܠܐ ܗܘܝܬܘܢ ܡܦܩܩܝܢ ܐܝܟ ܚܢܦܐ ܣܒܪܝܢ ܓܝܪ ܕܒܡܡܠܠܐ ܣܓܝܐܐ ܡܫܬܡܥܝܢ
verses.el: Προσευχόμενοι δὲ μὴ βατταλογήσητε ὥσπερ οἱ ἐθνικοί· δοκοῦσιν γὰρ ὅτι ἐν τῇ πολυλογίᾳ αὐτῶν εἰσακουσθήσονται.
verses.en: And when you pray, do not babble like the pagans, for they suppose that in much speaking they will be heard.
verses.ar: وإذا صَلَّيتُم لا تُرَدِّدوا كالأُمَمِ الأجانِب، فإنَّهُم يَحسَبونَ أنَّهُم بِكَثرةِ كَلامِهِم يُستَجابُ لَهُم.

verses.syr: ܠܐ ܗܟܝܠ ܬܕܡܘܢ ܠܗܘܢ ܐܒܘܟܘܢ ܓܝܪ ܝܕܥ ܡܢܐ ܡܬܒܥܐ ܠܟܘܢ ܥܕܠܐ ܬܫܐܠܘܢܝܗܝ
verses.el: μὴ οὖν ὁμοιωθῆτε αὐτοῖς· οἶδεν γὰρ ὁ Πατὴρ ὑμῶν ὧν χρείαν ἔχετε πρὸ τοῦ ὑμᾶς αἰτῆσαι αὐτόν.
verses.en: Therefore do not be like them, for your Father knows what you need before you ask Him.
verses.ar: فلا تَكونوا مِثلَهُم، لأنَّ أباكُم يَعلَمُ ما تَحتاجونَ إليهِ قَبلَ أن تَسأَلوه.

verses.syr: ܗܟܢܐ ܗܟܝܠ ܨܠܘ ܐܢܬܘܢ ܐܒܘܢ ܕܒܫܡܝܐ ܢܬܩܕܫ ܫܡܟ
verses.el: οὕτως οὖν προσεύχεσθε ὑμεῖς Πάτερ ἡμῶν ὁ ἐν τοῖς οὐρανοῖς· Ἁγιασθήτω τὸ ὄνομά σου·
verses.en: Thus therefore pray: Our Father who is in the heavens, hallowed be Your name.
verses.ar: فهكذا صَلُّوا: أبانا الذي في السَّماوات، لِيَتَقَدَّس اسمُك.

verses.syr: ܬܐܬܐ ܡܠܟܘܬܟ ܢܗܘܐ ܨܒܝܢܟ ܐܝܟܢܐ ܕܒܫܡܝܐ ܐܦ ܒܐܪܥܐ
verses.el: ἐλθάτω ἡ βασιλεία σου· γενηθήτω τὸ θέλημά σου, ὡς ἐν οὐρανῷ καὶ ἐπὶ γῆς·
verses.en: Let Your sovereignty come. Let Your will be done, as in the heavens so also on the earth.
verses.ar: لِيَأتِ مَلَكوتُك. لِتَكُن مَشيئَتُك كما في السَّماءِ كذلكَ على الأرض.

verses.syr: ܗܒ ܠܢ ܠܚܡܐ ܕܣܘܢܩܢܢ ܝܘܡܢܐ
verses.el: Τὸν ἄρτον ἡμῶν τὸν ἐπιούσιον δὸς ἡμῖν σήμερον·
verses.en: Give us the bread of our need today.
verses.ar: خُبزَ حاجَتِنا أعطِنا اليوم.

verses.syr: ܘܫܒܘܩ ܠܢ ܚܘܒܝܢ ܐܝܟܢܐ ܕܐܦ ܚܢܢ ܫܒܩܢ ܠܚܝܒܝܢ
verses.el: καὶ ἄφες ἡμῖν τὰ ὀφειλήματα ἡμῶν, ὡς καὶ ἡμεῖς ἀφήκαμεν τοῖς ὀφειλέταις ἡμῶν·
verses.en: And release us our debts, as we also have released those indebted to us.
verses.ar: وأطلِق لَنا ذُنوبَنا كما أطلَقنا لِلمُذنِبينَ إلَينا.

verses.syr: ܘܠܐ ܬܥܠܢ ܠܢܣܝܘܢܐ ܐܠܐ ܦܨܢ ܡܢ ܒܝܫܐ ܡܛܠ ܕܕܝܠܟ ܗܝ ܡܠܟܘܬܐ ܘܚܝܠܐ ܘܬܫܒܘܚܬܐ ܠܥܠܡ ܥܠܡܝܢ
verses.el: καὶ μὴ εἰσενέγκῃς ἡμᾶς εἰς πειρασμόν, ἀλλὰ ῥῦσαι ἡμᾶς ἀπὸ τοῦ πονηροῦ.
verses.en: And do not bring us into testing, but deliver us from the evil one. For Yours is the sovereignty, and the power, and the glory, unto the age of ages.
verses.ar: ولا تُدخِلنا في التَّجرِبة، لكِن نَجِّنا مِن الشَّرير. فإنَّ لَكَ المَلَكوتَ والقُدرةَ والمَجدَ إلى أبَدِ الآبِدين.

verses.syr: ܐܢ ܓܝܪ ܬܫܒܩܘܢ ܠܒܢܝܢܫܐ ܣܟܠܘܬܗܘܢ ܢܫܒܘܩ ܐܦ ܠܟܘܢ ܐܒܘܟܘܢ ܕܒܫܡܝܐ
verses.el: Ἐὰν γὰρ ἀφῆτε τοῖς ἀνθρώποις τὰ παραπτώματα αὐτῶν, ἀφήσει καὶ ὑμῖν ὁ Πατὴρ ὑμῶν ὁ οὐράνιος·
verses.en: For if you release unto the sons of men their offenses, your Father who is in the heavens shall also release unto you.
verses.ar: لأنَّكُم إن أطلَقتُم لِبَني البَشَرِ زَلّاتِهِم، يُطلِقُ لَكُم أبوكُمُ السَّماوِيُّ أيضاً.

verses.syr: ܐܢ ܕܝܢ ܠܐ ܬܫܒܩܘܢ ܠܒܢܝܢܫܐ ܐܦܠܐ ܐܒܘܟܘܢ ܫܒܩ ܠܟܘܢ ܣܟܠܘܬܟܘܢ
verses.el: ἐὰν δὲ μὴ ἀφῆτε τοῖς ἀνθρώποις, οὐδὲ ὁ Πατὴρ ὑμῶν ἀφήσει τὰ παραπτώματα ὑμῶν.
verses.en: But if you do not release unto the sons of men, neither shall your Father release your offenses.
verses.ar: وإن لَم تُطلِقوا لِبَني البَشَر، فأبوكُم أيضاً لا يُطلِقُ لَكُم زَلّاتِكُم.

verses.syr: ܐܡܬܝ ܕܝܢ ܕܨܝܡܝܢ ܐܢܬܘܢ ܠܐ ܬܗܘܘܢ ܟܡܝܪܐ ܐܝܟ ܢܣܒܝ ܒܐܦܐ ܡܚܒܠܝܢ ܓܝܪ ܦܪܨܘܦܝܗܘܢ ܐܝܟ ܕܢܬܚܙܘܢ ܠܒܢܝܢܫܐ ܕܨܝܡܝܢ ܘܐܡܝܢ ܐܡܪ ܐܢܐ ܠܟܘܢ ܕܩܒܠܘ ܐܓܪܗܘܢ
verses.el: Ὅταν δὲ νηστεύητε, μὴ γίνεσθε ὡς οἱ ὑποκριταὶ σκυθρωποί· ἀφανίζουσιν γὰρ τὰ πρόσωπα αὐτῶν ὅπως φανῶσιν τοῖς ἀνθρώποις νηστεύοντες· ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν, ἀπέχουσιν τὸν μισθὸν αὐτῶν.
verses.en: And when you fast, be not gloomy like the face-takers, for they disfigure their faces so that they may be seen by the sons of men as fasting. Amen I say to you: they have received their reward.
verses.ar: وإذا صُمتُم، فلا تَكونوا عابِسينَ كَآخِذي الوُجوه، فإنَّهُم يُشَوِّهونَ وُجوهَهُم لِيَتَراءَوا لِلنّاسِ صائِمين. الحَقَّ أقولُ لَكُم: قَد استَوفَوا أجرَهُم.

verses.syr: ܐܢܬ ܕܝܢ ܡܐ ܕܨܐܡ ܐܢܬ ܐܫܝܓ ܐܦܝܟ ܘܡܫܘܚ ܪܫܟ
verses.el: σὺ δὲ νηστεύων ἄλειψαί σου τὴν κεφαλὴν καὶ τὸ πρόσωπόν σου νίψαι,
verses.en: But you, when you fast, wash your face and anoint your head,
verses.ar: بل أنتَ إذا صُمتَ، فاغسِل وَجهَكَ وادهُن رَأسَك،

verses.syr: ܐܝܟ ܕܠܐ ܬܬܚܙܐ ܠܒܢܝܢܫܐ ܕܨܐܡ ܐܢܬ ܐܠܐ ܠܐܒܘܟ ܕܒܟܣܝܐ ܘܐܒܘܟ ܕܚܙܐ ܒܟܣܝܐ ܗܘ ܢܦܪܥܟ
verses.el: ὅπως μὴ φανῇς τοῖς ἀνθρώποις νηστεύων ἀλλὰ τῷ Πατρί σου τῷ ἐν τῷ κρυφαίῳ· καὶ ὁ Πατήρ σου ὁ βλέπων ἐν τῷ κρυφαίῳ ἀποδώσει σοι.
verses.en: so that you appear not unto the sons of men as fasting, but unto your Father who is in the hidden; and your Father who sees in the hidden — He shall repay you.
verses.ar: حتّى لا تَتَراءى لِلنّاسِ صائِماً بل لأبيكَ الذي في الخَفاء؛ وأبوكَ الذي يَرى في الخَفاءِ هو يُجازيك.

verses.syr: ܠܐ ܬܣܝܡܘܢ ܠܟܘܢ ܣܝܡܬܐ ܒܐܪܥܐ ܐܬܪ ܕܣܣܐ ܘܐܟܠܐ ܡܚܒܠܝܢ ܘܐܝܟܐ ܕܓܢܒܐ ܦܠܫܝܢ ܘܓܢܒܝܢ
verses.el: Μὴ θησαυρίζετε ὑμῖν θησαυροὺς ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς, ὅπου σὴς καὶ βρῶσις ἀφανίζει, καὶ ὅπου κλέπται διορύσσουσιν καὶ κλέπτουσιν·
verses.en: Do not lay up for yourselves treasure on the earth, where moth and devouring-thing corrupt, and where thieves dig through and steal.
verses.ar: لا تَكتَنِزوا لأنفُسِكُم كُنوزاً على الأرض، حَيثُ تُفسِدُها السُّوسةُ والعَثّ، وحَيثُ يَنقُبُ اللُّصوصُ ويَسرِقون.

verses.syr: ܐܠܐ ܣܝܡܘ ܠܟܘܢ ܣܝܡܬܐ ܒܫܡܝܐ ܐܝܟܐ ܕܠܐ ܣܣܐ ܘܠܐ ܐܟܠܐ ܡܚܒܠܝܢ ܘܐܝܟܐ ܕܓܢܒܐ ܠܐ ܦܠܫܝܢ ܘܠܐ ܓܢܒܝܢ
verses.el: θησαυρίζετε δὲ ὑμῖν θησαυροὺς ἐν οὐρανῷ, ὅπου οὔτε σὴς οὔτε βρῶσις ἀφανίζει, καὶ ὅπου κλέπται οὐ διορύσσουσιν οὐδὲ κλέπτουσιν·
verses.en: But lay up for yourselves treasure in the heavens, where neither moth nor devouring-thing corrupt, and where thieves do not dig through nor steal.
verses.ar: بل اكتَنِزوا لأنفُسِكُم كُنوزاً في السَّماوات، حَيثُ لا تُفسِدُها السُّوسةُ والعَثّ، وحَيثُ لا يَنقُبُ اللُّصوصُ ولا يَسرِقون.

verses.syr: ܐܝܟܐ ܓܝܪ ܕܐܝܬܝܗ ܣܝܡܬܟܘܢ ܬܡܢ ܗܘ ܐܦ ܠܒܟܘܢ
verses.el: ὅπου γάρ ἐστιν ὁ θησαυρός σου, ἐκεῖ ἔσται καὶ ἡ καρδία σου.
verses.en: For where your treasure is, there also is your heart.
verses.ar: لأنَّهُ حَيثُ كنزُكَ، هُناكَ يَكونُ قَلبُكَ أيضاً.

verses.syr: ܫܪܓܐ ܕܦܓܪܐ ܐܝܬܝܗ ܥܝܢܐ ܐܢ ܥܝܢܟ ܗܟܝܠ ܬܗܘܐ ܦܫܝܛܐ ܐܦ ܟܠܗ ܦܓܪܟ ܢܗܝܪ ܗܘ
verses.el: Ὁ λύχνος τοῦ σώματός ἐστιν ὁ ὀφθαλμός. ἐὰν οὖν ᾖ ὁ ὀφθαλμός σου ἁπλοῦς, ὅλον τὸ σῶμά σου φωτεινὸν ἔσται·
verses.en: The lamp of the body is the eye. If then your eye be simple, your whole body also is luminous.
verses.ar: سِراجُ الجَسَدِ هو العَين. فإن كانَت عَينُكَ سَليمةً، فجَسَدُكَ كُلُّهُ نَيِّر.

verses.syr: ܐܢ ܕܝܢ ܥܝܢܟ ܬܗܘܐ ܒܝܫܐ ܟܠܗ ܦܓܪܟ ܚܫܘܟܐ ܢܗܘܐ ܐܢ ܗܟܝܠ ܢܘܗܪܐ ܕܒܟ ܚܫܘܟܐ ܗܘ ܚܫܘܟܟ ܟܡܐ ܢܗܘܐ
verses.el: ἐὰν δὲ ὁ ὀφθαλμός σου πονηρὸς ᾖ, ὅλον τὸ σῶμά σου σκοτεινὸν ἔσται. εἰ οὖν τὸ φῶς τὸ ἐν σοὶ σκότος ἐστίν, τὸ σκότος πόσον.
verses.en: But if your eye be evil, your whole body shall be dark. If then the light that is in you is darkness — how great is your darkness!
verses.ar: وإن كانَت عَينُكَ شِرِّيرة، فجَسَدُكَ كُلُّهُ مُظلِم. فإن كانَ النّورُ الذي فيكَ ظَلاماً — فكَم يَكونُ ظَلامُهُ!

verses.syr: ܠܐ ܐܢܫ ܡܫܟܚ ܠܬܪܝܢ ܡܪܘܢ ܠܡܦܠܚ ܐܘ ܓܝܪ ܠܚܕ ܢܣܢܐ ܘܠܐܚܪܢܐ ܢܪܚܡ ܐܘ ܠܚܕ ܢܝܩܪ ܘܠܐܚܪܢܐ ܢܫܘܛ ܠܐ ܡܫܟܚܝܢ ܐܢܬܘܢ ܠܐܠܗܐ ܠܡܦܠܚ ܘܠܡܡܘܢܐ
verses.el: Οὐδεὶς δύναται δυσὶ κυρίοις δουλεύειν· ἢ γὰρ τὸν ἕνα μισήσει καὶ τὸν ἕτερον ἀγαπήσει, ἢ ἑνὸς ἀνθέξεται καὶ τοῦ ἑτέρου καταφρονήσει· οὐ δύνασθε Θεῷ δουλεύειν καὶ μαμωνᾷ.
verses.en: No man can serve two masters: for either the one he will hate and the other he will love, or the one he will honor and the other he will despise. You cannot serve God and Mammon.
verses.ar: لا يَقدِرُ أحَدٌ أن يَخدُمَ سَيِّدَينِ: لأنَّهُ إمّا أن يُبغِضَ أحَدَهُما ويُحِبَّ الآخَر، أو يَتَمَسَّكَ بِأحَدِهِما ويَستَهينَ بِالآخَر. لا تَقدِرونَ أن تَخدُموا اللهَ والمال.

verses.syr: ܡܛܠ ܗܢܐ ܐܡܪ ܐܢܐ ܠܟܘܢ ܠܐ ܬܐܨܦܘܢ ܠܢܦܫܟܘܢ ܡܢܐ ܬܐܟܠܘܢ ܘܡܢܐ ܬܫܬܘܢ ܘܠܐ ܠܦܓܪܟܘܢ ܡܢܐ ܬܠܒܫܘܢ ܠܐ ܗܐ ܢܦܫܐ ܝܬܝܪܐ ܡܢ ܣܝܒܪܬܐ ܘܦܓܪܐ ܡܢ ܠܒܘܫܐ
verses.el: Διὰ τοῦτο λέγω ὑμῖν, μὴ μεριμνᾶτε τῇ ψυχῇ ὑμῶν τί φάγητε ἢ τί πίητε, μηδὲ τῷ σώματι ὑμῶν τί ἐνδύσησθε· οὐχὶ ἡ ψυχὴ πλεῖόν ἐστιν τῆς τροφῆς καὶ τὸ σῶμα τοῦ ἐνδύματος;
verses.en: Therefore I say to you: do not be anxious for your breath — what you shall eat and what you shall drink — nor for your body, what you shall wear. Is not the breath more than sustenance, and the body more than clothing?
verses.ar: لِذلكَ أقولُ لَكُم: لا تَهتَمُّوا لِنُفوسِكُم — بِما تَأكُلونَ وما تَشرَبون — ولا لأجسادِكُم بِما تَلبَسون. أَلَيسَت النَّفسُ أكثَرَ مِن الطَّعام، والجَسَدُ أكثَرَ مِن اللِّباس؟

verses.syr: ܚܘܪܘ ܒܦܪܚܬܐ ܕܫܡܝܐ ܕܠܐ ܙܪܥܝܢ ܘܠܐ ܚܨܕܝܢ ܘܠܐ ܚܡܠܝܢ ܒܐܘܨܪܐ ܘܐܒܘܟܘܢ ܕܒܫܡܝܐ ܡܬܪܣܐ ܠܗܘܢ ܠܐ ܗܐ ܐܢܬܘܢ ܡܝܬܪܝܢ ܐܢܬܘܢ ܡܢܗܘܢ
verses.el: ἐμβλέψατε εἰς τὰ πετεινὰ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ, ὅτι οὐ σπείρουσιν οὐδὲ θερίζουσιν οὐδὲ συνάγουσιν εἰς ἀποθήκας, καὶ ὁ Πατὴρ ὑμῶν ὁ οὐράνιος τρέφει αὐτά· οὐχ ὑμεῖς μᾶλλον διαφέρετε αὐτῶν;
verses.en: Look at the birds of the heavens: they do not sow, they do not reap, they do not gather into storehouses — and your Father who is in the heavens sustains them. Are you not much more excellent than they?
verses.ar: انظُروا إلى طُيورِ السَّماء: إنَّها لا تَزرَعُ ولا تَحصُد ولا تَجمَعُ في مَخازِن — وأبوكُمُ السَّماوِيُّ يَقوتُها. أَلَستُم أنتُم أفضَلَ مِنها بِكَثير؟

verses.syr: ܡܢܘ ܕܝܢ ܡܢܟܘܢ ܟܕ ܝܨܦ ܡܫܟܚ ܠܡܘܣܦܘ ܥܠ ܩܘܡܬܗ ܐܡܬܐ ܚܕܐ
verses.el: τίς δὲ ἐξ ὑμῶν μεριμνῶν δύναται προσθεῖναι ἐπὶ τὴν ἡλικίαν αὐτοῦ πῆχυν ἕνα;
verses.en: And which of you, by being anxious, can add unto his stature one cubit?
verses.ar: وأيُّكُم إذا اهتَمَّ يَقدِرُ أن يَزيدَ على قامَتِهِ ذِراعاً واحِداً؟

verses.syr: ܘܥܠ ܠܒܘܫܐ ܡܢܐ ܝܨܦܝܢ ܐܢܬܘܢ ܐܬܒܩܘ ܒܫܘܫܢܐ ܕܕܒܪܐ ܐܝܟܢܐ ܪܒܝܢ ܕܠܐ ܠܐܝܢ ܘܠܐ ܥܙܠܢ
verses.el: καὶ περὶ ἐνδύματος τί μεριμνᾶτε; καταμάθετε τὰ κρίνα τοῦ ἀγροῦ πῶς αὐξάνουσιν· οὐ κοπιῶσιν οὐδὲ νήθουσιν·
verses.en: And about clothing, why are you anxious? Consider the lilies of the field, how they grow: they do not labor and they do not spin.
verses.ar: وعَن اللِّباسِ لِماذا تَهتَمُّون؟ تَأَمَّلوا زَنابِقَ الحَقلِ كَيفَ تَنمو: لا تَتعَبُ ولا تَغزِل.

verses.syr: ܐܡܪ ܐܢܐ ܠܟܘܢ ܕܝܢ ܕܐܦܠܐ ܫܠܝܡܘܢ ܒܟܠܗ ܫܘܒܚܗ ܐܬܟܣܝ ܐܝܟ ܚܕܐ ܡܢܗܝܢ
verses.el: λέγω δὲ ὑμῖν ὅτι οὐδὲ Σολομὼν ἐν πάσῃ τῇ δόξῃ αὐτοῦ περιεβάλετο ὡς ἓν τούτων.
verses.en: But I say to you that not even Shelomoh in all his weight was clothed like one of these.
verses.ar: لكِنِّي أقولُ لَكُم: إنَّ سُليمانَ في كُلِّ بَهائِهِ لم يَلبَس كَواحِدةٍ مِنها.

verses.syr: ܐܢ ܕܝܢ ܠܥܡܝܪܐ ܕܚܩܠܐ ܕܝܘܡܢܐ ܐܝܬܘܗܝ ܘܡܚܪ ܢܦܠ ܒܬܢܘܪܐ ܐܠܗܐ ܗܟܢܐ ܡܠܒܫ ܠܐ ܣܓܝ ܝܬܝܪ ܠܟܘܢ ܙܥܘܪܝ ܗܝܡܢܘܬܐ
verses.el: εἰ δὲ τὸν χόρτον τοῦ ἀγροῦ σήμερον ὄντα καὶ αὔριον εἰς κλίβανον βαλλόμενον ὁ Θεὸς οὕτως ἀμφιέννυσιν, οὐ πολλῷ μᾶλλον ὑμᾶς, ὀλιγόπιστοι;
verses.en: And if the grass of the field, which today exists and tomorrow falls into the oven, God so clothes — how much more you, O small of trust!
verses.ar: فإن كانَ اللهُ يَكسو هكذا عُشبَ الحَقلِ الذي اليَومَ مَوجودٌ وغَداً يُطرَحُ في التَّنُّور — أفلا بِالأَولى يَكسوكُم أنتُم، يا قَليلي الإيمان؟

verses.syr: ܠܐ ܗܟܝܠ ܬܐܨܦܘܢ ܐܘ ܬܐܡܪܘܢ ܡܢܐ ܢܐܟܘܠ ܐܘ ܡܢܐ ܢܫܬܐ ܐܘ ܡܢܐ ܢܬܟܣܐ
verses.el: μὴ οὖν μεριμνήσητε λέγοντες· Τί φάγωμεν; ἤ· Τί πίωμεν; ἤ· Τί περιβαλώμεθα;
verses.en: Therefore do not be anxious, saying: "What shall we eat?" or "What shall we drink?" or "What shall we wear?"
verses.ar: فلا تَهتَمُّوا قائِلين: «ماذا نَأكُل؟» أو «ماذا نَشرَب؟» أو «بِماذا نَلبَس؟»

verses.syr: ܟܠܗܝܢ ܓܝܪ ܗܠܝܢ ܥܡܡܐ ܗܘ ܒܥܝܢ ܠܗܝܢ ܐܒܘܟܘܢ ܕܝܢ ܕܒܫܡܝܐ ܝܕܥ ܕܐܦ ܠܟܘܢ ܡܬܒܥܝܢ ܗܠܝܢ ܟܠܗܝܢ
verses.el: πάντα γὰρ ταῦτα τὰ ἔθνη ἐπιζητοῦσιν· οἶδεν γὰρ ὁ Πατὴρ ὑμῶν ὁ οὐράνιος ὅτι χρῄζετε τούτων ἁπάντων.
verses.en: For all these things the nations seek. But your Father who is in the heavens knows that you also need all these things.
verses.ar: فإنَّ هذهِ كُلَّها الأُمَمُ تَطلُبُها. وأبوكُمُ السَّماوِيُّ يَعلَمُ أنَّكُم تَحتاجونَ إلى هذهِ كُلِّها.

verses.syr: ܒܥܘ ܕܝܢ ܠܘܩܕܡ ܡܠܟܘܬܗ ܕܐܠܗܐ ܘܙܕܝܩܘܬܗ ܘܟܠܗܝܢ ܗܠܝܢ ܡܬܬܘܣܦܢ ܠܟܘܢ
verses.el: ζητεῖτε δὲ πρῶτον τὴν βασιλείαν καὶ τὴν δικαιοσύνην αὐτοῦ, καὶ ταῦτα πάντα προστεθήσεται ὑμῖν.
verses.en: But seek first the sovereignty of God and His rightness, and all these things shall be added unto you.
verses.ar: لكِن اطلُبوا أوَّلاً مَلَكوتَ اللهِ وبِرَّهُ، فهذهِ كُلُّها تُزادُ لَكُم.

verses.syr: ܠܐ ܗܟܝܠ ܬܐܨܦܘܢ ܕܡܚܪ ܗܘ ܓܝܪ ܡܚܪ ܝܨܦ ܕܝܠܗ ܣܦܩ ܠܗ ܠܝܘܡܐ ܒܝܫܬܗ
verses.el: μὴ οὖν μεριμνήσητε εἰς τὴν αὔριον, ἡ γὰρ αὔριον μεριμνήσει ἑαυτῆς· ἀρκετὸν τῇ ἡμέρᾳ ἡ κακία αὐτῆς.
verses.en: Therefore do not be anxious about tomorrow, for tomorrow shall be anxious for itself. Sufficient unto the day is its own trouble.
verses.ar: فلا تَهتَمُّوا لِلغَد، لأنَّ الغَدَ يَهتَمُّ بِشُؤونِهِ. كافٍ لِليَومِ شَرُّهُ.

chapters.commentary:
# Annotations on Matthew Chapter 6 — The Prayer of the Anointed

## Syriac-First Approach

This chapter is translated primarily from the Syriac Peshitta, with Greek noted where the two diverge. The Peshitta preserves Aramaic — the language Jesus actually spoke. The Greek New Testament is already a translation; the Syriac may be closer to the original words.

## Root-Recovery Choices

### v.1: ܙܕܩܬܐ (zedqta) — "rightness-deed"
Syriac root ܙ-ܕ-ܩ (z-d-q): rightness, justice, almsgiving. The same root as Hebrew צדק (tsedeq) and Arabic صدق (sidq). In Aramaic, "doing rightness" naturally includes almsgiving — the act of giving IS the act of rightness. Greek uses δικαιοσύνη (dikaiosynē, "righteousness") in v.1 but then switches to ἐλεημοσύνη (eleēmosynē, "alms/mercy") in v.2-4. The Syriac uses ܙܕܩܬܐ consistently throughout — no split between abstract "righteousness" and concrete "charity." The root-recovery translation follows the Syriac: "rightness-deed" throughout.

### v.2, 5, 16: ܢܣܒܝ ܒܐܦܐ (nasḇay b-ape) — "face-takers"
Literally "takers of faces" — those who put on a face, who take on an appearance. This is far more vivid than the Greek ὑποκριταί (hypokritai, "actors/pretenders"), which comes from theater. The Syriac metaphor is concrete and physical: they TAKE a face that isn't theirs. The Hebrew equivalent would be נשׂאי פנים (nos'ei fanim). The Arabic cognate وجه (wajh, "face") shares the Semitic root.

### v.9: ܐܒܘܢ ܕܒܫܡܝܐ (avun d-va-shmaya) — "Our Father who is in the heavens"
This is the Lord's Prayer in the language Jesus spoke. ܐܒܘܢ (avun) = "our father" — cognate with Hebrew אבינו (avinu) and Arabic أبونا (abuna). ܫܡܝܐ (shmaya) = "heavens" — cognate with Hebrew שמים (shamayim) and Arabic سماء (samā'). The Greek Πάτερ ἡμῶν matches exactly; no divergence here.

### v.9: ܢܬܩܕܫ ܫܡܟ (netqaddash shmakh) — "hallowed be Your name"
Root ܩ-ܕ-ܫ (q-d-sh): holy, set apart. Cognate with Hebrew קדש (qodesh) and Arabic قدس (quds, as in القدس al-Quds/Jerusalem). The Syriac ܢܬܩܕܫ is the Ethpael (reflexive/passive) — "let Your name BE made holy." Greek ἁγιασθήτω is a faithful equivalent.

### v.10: ܡܠܟܘܬܟ (malkutkh) — "Your sovereignty"
Root ܡ-ܠ-ܟ (m-l-k): to rule, to reign, sovereignty. Cognate with Hebrew מלכות (malkhut) and Arabic ملكوت (malakūt) — the exact word used in the Qur'an for God's dominion (36:83: سبحان الذي بيده ملكوت كل شيء, "Glory to Him in whose hand is the sovereignty of all things"). NOT "kingdom" — which implies a territory. This is SOVEREIGNTY, the act and authority of ruling. The Greek βασιλεία can mean both, but the Syriac is unambiguous.

### v.10: ܨܒܝܢܟ (tsevyonakh) — "Your will/desire"
Root ܨ-ܒ-ܝ (ts-b-y): to will, to desire, to delight in. The Syriac word carries a sense of DESIRE and DELIGHT, not merely cold will. Greek θέλημα is more neutral. The Syriac suggests: "let what DELIGHTS You come to be."

### v.11: ܠܚܡܐ ܕܣܘܢܩܢܢ (laḥma d-sunqanan) — "the bread of our need"
**THIS IS THE KEY DIVERGENCE.** The Greek has τὸν ἄρτον ἡμῶν τὸν ἐπιούσιον — and ἐπιούσιον (epiousios) is a word that appears NOWHERE ELSE in all of Greek literature. Scholars have debated its meaning for two millennia: "daily"? "supersubstantial"? "for the coming day"? "beyond-being"?

The Syriac cuts through all ambiguity: ܕܣܘܢܩܢܢ (d-sunqanan) = "of our NEED." Simple, concrete, Aramaic. Give us the bread that we need. Not mystical bread, not bread-beyond-essence, not bread for some future day — the bread of our actual need, today.

This strongly suggests that Jesus said something straightforward in Aramaic, and the Greek translator coined an unusual word (or borrowed an obscure one) trying to render it. The Syriac preserves the concrete Aramaic original.

ܠܚܡܐ (laḥma, "bread") is cognate with Hebrew לחם (leḥem) and Arabic لحم (laḥm — which shifted to mean "meat" in Arabic, but the root is shared).

### v.12: ܚܘܒܝܢ (ḥawbayn) — "our debts"
Root ܚ-ܘ-ܒ (ḥ-w-b): debt, guilt, obligation. The Syriac word carries both financial and moral debt — what you owe. Greek ὀφειλήματα (opheilēmata) matches well ("debts/obligations"). But note that Luke's version of the prayer (11:4) uses the Greek ἁμαρτίας ("sins/missing-the-mark") where Matthew uses ὀφειλήματα — the Syriac ܚܘܒܝܢ covers both senses naturally.

### v.12: ܫܒܘܩ (shvoq) — "release"
Root ܫ-ܒ-ܩ (sh-b-q): to leave, to release, to let go, to forgive. This is the word Jesus cried from the cross: ܫܒܘܩ ܠܗܘܢ (shvoq l-hun, "release them" — Luke 23:34). It means to physically LET GO — not an abstract "forgiveness" but a concrete releasing of what is held against someone. Cognate with Hebrew שבק and Arabic ترك (in sense). Greek ἄφες (aphes) from ἀφίημι has a similar concrete sense ("send away").

### v.13: ܢܣܝܘܢܐ (nesyona) — "testing"
Root ܢ-ܣ-ܝ (n-s-y): to test, to try, to prove. Cognate with Hebrew נסיון (nisayon) and Arabic root ن-س-ي. Greek πειρασμός (peirasmos) means both "temptation" and "testing." The Syriac is closer to "testing/trial" than "temptation" — do not bring us into the place of being tested beyond our capacity.

### v.13: ܒܝܫܐ (bisha) — "the evil one"
Syriac ܒܝܫܐ is unambiguously "the evil ONE" (masculine noun), not abstract "evil." Greek τοῦ πονηροῦ is ambiguous (neuter or masculine). The Syriac resolves the ambiguity: deliver us from the evil one — a personal adversary.

### v.13: ܠܥܠܡ ܥܠܡܝܢ (l-ʿalam ʿalmin) — "unto the age of ages"
The doxology ("For Yours is the sovereignty...") is PRESENT in the Syriac and in many Greek manuscripts but absent from the earliest Greek texts. The Syriac tradition consistently includes it. ܥܠܡ (ʿalam) is cognate with Hebrew עולם (olam, "the hidden age") and Arabic عالم (ʿālam). "For ever and ever" obscures the root — "unto the age of ages" preserves it.

### v.22: ܦܫܝܛܐ (pshiṭa) — "simple/single"
Root ܦ-ܫ-ܛ (p-sh-ṭ): simple, spread out, plain, generous. This is the same word as the name of the Syriac Bible itself — the Peshitta, "the Simple One." A "simple eye" is one that sees plainly, without duplicity — and in Aramaic also implies GENEROSITY (an open hand spread out). Greek ἁπλοῦς (haplous) carries similar double meaning. The opposite is ܒܝܫܐ (bisha, "evil") — the same word as in v.13.

### v.24: ܡܡܘܢܐ (mamona) — "Mammon"
An Aramaic word that passed directly into the Greek as μαμωνᾷ (mamōna). This is one of the few words that proves the Greek is translating from an Aramaic original — the Greek simply borrows the Aramaic word untranslated. Root meaning: wealth, riches, that in which one puts trust. Related to Hebrew/Aramaic אמן (aman, "to trust") — Mammon is that in which you place your trust instead of God.

### v.25: ܢܦܫܐ (nafsha) — "breath/life"
Root ܢ-ܦ-ܫ (n-f-sh): same root as Hebrew נפש (nefesh) and Arabic نفس (nafs). "Do not be anxious for your BREATH" — not "soul" or "life" in the abstract, but the breath that sustains you. Greek ψυχή (psychē) already tilts toward "soul" as a Greek philosophical concept. The Syriac keeps it grounded in the body.

### v.29: ܫܘܒܚܗ (shuvḥeh) — "his glory/weight"
Root ܫ-ܒ-ḥ (sh-b-ḥ): to praise, to glorify. Rendered as "weight" following the Hebrew root-recovery approach where כבוד (kavod) = weight/heaviness. Greek δόξα (doxa) = opinion/glory. The root-recovery reaches behind both to: Solomon in all his WEIGHT/SPLENDOR was not clothed like one of these.

### v.30: ܙܥܘܪܝ ܗܝܡܢܘܬܐ (z'uray haymanutha) — "small of trust"
ܗܝܡܢܘܬܐ (haymanutha): trust, faithfulness. Root ܗ-ܝ-ܡ-ܢ (h-y-m-n), cognate with Hebrew אמן/אמונה (emunah) and Arabic إيمان (iman). NOT "faith" as belief in propositions — but TRUST, the firm standing. "O you of little TRUST" — you who do not stand firm. Greek ὀλιγόπιστοι (oligopistoi) is a compound: "small-trust."

### v.33: ܡܠܟܘܬܗ ܕܐܠܗܐ ܘܙܕܝܩܘܬܗ — "the sovereignty of God and His rightness"
ܡܠܟܘܬܐ (malkutha) + ܙܕܝܩܘܬܐ (zaddiqutha): sovereignty + rightness. Two key root-recovery terms in one verse. Greek: τὴν βασιλείαν καὶ τὴν δικαιοσύνην. Both Syriac roots have direct Hebrew and Arabic cognates: מלכות/ملكوت and צדק/صدق.

## Cross-Scriptural Root Connections

| Syriac | Hebrew | Arabic | Shared meaning | Qur'an connection |
|--------|--------|--------|---------------|-------------------|
| ܡܠܟܘܬܐ (malkutha) | מלכות (malkhut) | ملكوت (malakūt) | sovereignty/kingdom | 36:83 — ملكوت كل شيء |
| ܩܕܫ (qaddash) | קדש (qodesh) | قدس (quds) | holy/set apart | Throughout — القدوس (al-Quddūs) |
| ܫܡܝܐ (shmaya) | שמים (shamayim) | سماء (samā') | heavens | Throughout |
| ܐܒ (av) | אב (av) | أب (ab) | father | — |
| ܢܦܫ (nafsha) | נפש (nefesh) | نفس (nafs) | breath/self | 4:1, throughout |
| ܗܝܡܢ (hayman) | אמן (aman) | إيمان (imān) | trust/faith | 2:3, throughout |
| ܙܕܩ (zedq) | צדק (tsedeq) | صدق (sidq) | rightness/truth | Throughout — الصدق |
| ܫܒܩ (shvaq) | שבק (shavaq) | — | release/forgive | — |
| ܠܚܡ (laḥm) | לחם (leḥem) | لحم (laḥm) | bread/meat | — |
| ܥܠܡ (ʿalam) | עולם (olam) | عالم (ʿālam) | age/hidden/world | Throughout |

## Bahá'í Interpretive Notes

Bahá'u'lláh teaches that prayer should be offered in a spirit of detachment and sincerity — precisely the teaching of verses 1-8. The emphasis on the "hidden" (ܟܣܝܐ, kasya) prayer before the Father "who sees in the hidden" resonates with the Bahá'í understanding that God knows the inner reality, not the outward show. 'Abdu'l-Bahá interprets "daily bread" (the bread of our need) as including spiritual sustenance — the Word of God that feeds the soul day by day. The prayer for sovereignty (ܡܠܟܘܬܐ) aligns with the Bahá'í teaching of the coming of God's sovereignty on earth through progressive revelation — each Manifestation bringing the divine will closer to earthly realization.

## Integrative Links

- **v.9 "Our Father in the heavens" ↔ Qur'an 1:2**: رب العالمين — "Sustainer of the worlds." Both prayers open by addressing God in relation to the heavens/worlds. The Syriac ܐܒ (father) is relational; the Arabic رب (sustainer) is functional — together they complete the picture.
- **v.10 "sovereignty" ↔ Qur'an 1:4**: مالك يوم الدين — the same root م-ل-ك appears in both the Lord's Prayer and the Fatihah. Both prayers invoke God's sovereignty.
- **v.11 "bread of our need" ↔ Qur'an 1:5**: إياك نستعين — "You alone we ask for help." Both prayers contain a petition for sustenance/help.
- **v.12 "release us our debts" ↔ Qur'an 1:6**: اهدنا الصراط المستقيم — "Guide us to the straight path." Both ask for moral restoration.
- **v.13 "deliver us from the evil one" ↔ Qur'an 113-114**: The Mu'awwidhatayn (refuge prayers) — "I seek refuge...from the evil of what He created" / "from the evil of the whisperer."
- **v.19-21 treasure in heaven ↔ Qur'an 87:16-17**: بل تؤثرون الحياة الدنيا والآخرة خير وأبقى — "You prefer the worldly life, but the hereafter is better and more lasting."
- **v.33 "seek first the sovereignty" ↔ Qur'an 1:5**: إياك نعبد — "You alone we serve." The priority of divine sovereignty over worldly anxiety.

chapters.title: Matthew — ܩܦܠܐܘܢ 7
chapters.subtitle: Matthew — Chapter 7

verses.syr: ܠܐ ܬܕܘܢܘܢ ܕܠܐ ܬܬܕܝܢܘܢ
verses.el: Μὴ κρίνετε, ἵνα μὴ κριθῆτε·
verses.en: Do not judge, lest you be judged.
verses.ar: لا تَحكُموا لِئَلّا تُحكَمَ عَليكُم.

verses.syr: ܒܕܝܢܐ ܓܝܪ ܕܕܝܢܝܢ ܐܢܬܘܢ ܬܬܕܝܢܘܢ ܘܒܟܝܠܬܐ ܕܡܟܝܠܝܢ ܐܢܬܘܢ ܡܬܬܟܝܠ ܠܟܘܢ
verses.el: ἐν ᾧ γὰρ κρίματι κρίνετε κριθήσεσθε, καὶ ἐν ᾧ μέτρῳ μετρεῖτε μετρηθήσεται ὑμῖν.
verses.en: For by the judgment with which you judge, you shall be judged; and by the measure with which you measure, it shall be measured to you.
verses.ar: لأنَّهُ بِالحُكمِ الذي بهِ تَحكُمونَ تُحكَمونَ؛ وبِالكَيلِ الذي بهِ تَكيلونَ يُكالُ لَكُم.

verses.syr: ܡܢܐ ܕܝܢ ܚܙܐ ܐܢܬ ܓܠܐ ܕܒܥܝܢܗ ܕܐܚܘܟ ܘܩܪܝܬܐ ܕܒܥܝܢܟ ܠܐ ܒܚܪ ܐܢܬ
verses.el: τί δὲ βλέπεις τὸ κάρφος τὸ ἐν τῷ ὀφθαλμῷ τοῦ ἀδελφοῦ σου, τὴν δὲ ἐν τῷ σῷ ὀφθαλμῷ δοκὸν οὐ κατανοεῖς;
verses.en: And why do you see the splinter in your brother's eye, but the beam in your own eye you do not consider?
verses.ar: ولِماذا تَنظُرُ القَذاةَ التي في عَينِ أخيك، وأمّا الخَشَبةُ التي في عَينِكَ فلا تَشعُرُ بِها؟

verses.syr: ܐܘ ܐܝܟܢܐ ܐܡܪ ܐܢܬ ܠܐܚܘܟ ܫܒܘܩ ܐܦܩ ܓܠܐ ܡܢ ܥܝܢܟ ܘܗܐ ܩܪܝܬܐ ܒܥܝܢܟ
verses.el: ἢ πῶς ἐρεῖς τῷ ἀδελφῷ σου· Ἄφες ἐκβάλω τὸ κάρφος ἐκ τοῦ ὀφθαλμοῦ σου, καὶ ἰδοὺ ἡ δοκὸς ἐν τῷ ὀφθαλμῷ σοῦ;
verses.en: Or how do you say to your brother: "Permit me to take the splinter from your eye" — and behold, a beam is in your own eye?
verses.ar: أو كَيفَ تَقولُ لأخيك: «دَعني أُخرِجِ القَذاةَ مِن عَينِك» — وها الخَشَبةُ في عَينِكَ؟

verses.syr: ܢܣܒ ܒܐܦܐ ܐܦܩ ܠܘܩܕܡ ܩܪܝܬܐ ܡܢ ܥܝܢܟ ܘܗܝܕܝܢ ܢܬܒܚܪ ܠܟ ܠܡܦܩܘ ܓܠܐ ܡܢ ܥܝܢܗ ܕܐܚܘܟ
verses.el: ὑποκριτά, ἔκβαλε πρῶτον ἐκ τοῦ ὀφθαλμοῦ σοῦ τὴν δοκόν, καὶ τότε διαβλέψεις ἐκβαλεῖν τὸ κάρφος ἐκ τοῦ ὀφθαλμοῦ τοῦ ἀδελφοῦ σου.
verses.en: Face-taker! First take the beam from your own eye, and then it shall be clear for you to take the splinter from your brother's eye.
verses.ar: يا مُرائي! أَخرِج أوَّلاً الخَشَبةَ مِن عَينِك، وحينَئذٍ تُبصِرُ لِتُخرِجَ القَذاةَ مِن عَينِ أخيك.

verses.syr: ܠܐ ܬܬܠܘܢ ܩܘܕܫܐ ܠܟܠܒܐ ܘܠܐ ܬܪܡܘܢ ܡܪܓܢܝܬܟܘܢ ܩܕܡ ܚܙܝܪܐ ܕܠܡܐ ܢܕܘܫܘܢ ܐܢܝܢ ܒܪܓܠܝܗܘܢ ܘܢܗܦܟܘܢ ܢܒܙܥܘܢܟܘܢ
verses.el: Μὴ δῶτε τὸ ἅγιον τοῖς κυσίν, μηδὲ βάλητε τοὺς μαργαρίτας ὑμῶν ἔμπροσθεν τῶν χοίρων, μή ποτε καταπατήσουσιν αὐτοὺς ἐν τοῖς ποσὶν αὐτῶν καὶ στραφέντες ῥήξωσιν ὑμᾶς.
verses.en: Do not give what is holy to the dogs, and do not cast your pearls before swine, lest they trample them with their feet and turn and tear you.
verses.ar: لا تُعطوا القُدُسَ لِلكِلاب، ولا تَرموا دُرَّكُم أمامَ الخَنازير، لِئَلّا تَطَأَهُ بِأقدامِها وتَنقَلِبَ وتُمَزِّقَكُم.

verses.syr: ܫܐܠܘ ܘܢܬܝܗܒ ܠܟܘܢ ܒܥܘ ܘܬܫܟܚܘܢ ܩܘܫܘ ܘܢܬܦܬܚ ܠܟܘܢ
verses.el: Αἰτεῖτε, καὶ δοθήσεται ὑμῖν· ζητεῖτε, καὶ εὑρήσετε· κρούετε, καὶ ἀνοιγήσεται ὑμῖν.
verses.en: Ask, and it shall be given to you; seek, and you shall find; knock, and it shall be opened to you.
verses.ar: اسألوا يُعطَ لَكُم؛ اطلُبوا تَجِدوا؛ اقرَعوا يُفتَح لَكُم.

verses.syr: ܟܠ ܓܝܪ ܕܫܐܠ ܢܣܒ ܘܕܒܥܐ ܡܫܟܚ ܘܠܐܝܢܐ ܕܢܩܫ ܡܬܦܬܚ ܠܗ
verses.el: πᾶς γὰρ ὁ αἰτῶν λαμβάνει καὶ ὁ ζητῶν εὑρίσκει καὶ τῷ κρούοντι ἀνοιγήσεται.
verses.en: For every one who asks receives, and he who seeks finds, and to him who knocks it shall be opened.
verses.ar: لأنَّ كُلَّ مَن يَسألُ يَنالُ، ومَن يَطلُبُ يَجِد، ومَن يَقرَعُ يُفتَحُ له.

verses.syr: ܐܘ ܡܢܘ ܡܢܟܘܢ ܓܒܪܐ ܕܢܫܐܠܝܘܗܝ ܒܪܗ ܠܚܡܐ ܠܡܐ ܟܐܦܐ ܡܘܫܛ ܠܗ
verses.el: ἢ τίς ἐστιν ἐξ ὑμῶν ἄνθρωπος, ὃν αἰτήσει ὁ υἱὸς αὐτοῦ ἄρτον, μὴ λίθον ἐπιδώσει αὐτῷ;
verses.en: Or which of you is a man whose son asks him for bread — would he hand him a stone?
verses.ar: أو أيُّكُم رَجُلٌ يَسألُهُ ابنُهُ خُبزاً — فهَل يُعطيهِ حَجَراً؟

verses.syr: ܘܐܢ ܢܘܢܐ ܢܫܐܠܝܘܗܝ ܠܡܐ ܚܘܝܐ ܡܘܫܛ ܠܗ
verses.el: ἢ καὶ ἰχθὺν αἰτήσει, μὴ ὄφιν ἐπιδώσει αὐτῷ;
verses.en: And if he asks for a fish — would he hand him a serpent?
verses.ar: وإن سَألَ سَمَكةً — فهَل يُعطيهِ حَيَّةً؟

verses.syr: ܘܐܢ ܗܟܝܠ ܐܢܬܘܢ ܕܒܝܫܐ ܐܢܬܘܢ ܝܕܥܝܢ ܐܢܬܘܢ ܡܘܗܒܬܐ ܛܒܬܐ ܠܡܬܠ ܠܒܢܝܟܘܢ ܟܡܐ ܝܬܝܪܐܝܬ ܐܒܘܟܘܢ ܕܒܫܡܝܐ ܢܬܠ ܛܒܬܐ ܠܐܝܠܝܢ ܕܫܐܠܝܢ ܠܗ
verses.el: εἰ οὖν ὑμεῖς πονηροὶ ὄντες οἴδατε δόματα ἀγαθὰ διδόναι τοῖς τέκνοις ὑμῶν, πόσῳ μᾶλλον ὁ Πατὴρ ὑμῶν ὁ ἐν τοῖς οὐρανοῖς δώσει ἀγαθὰ τοῖς αἰτοῦσιν αὐτόν.
verses.en: If therefore you, who are evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more shall your Father who is in the heavens give good things to those who ask Him!
verses.ar: فإن كُنتُم وأنتُم أشرارٌ تَعرِفونَ كَيفَ تُعطونَ أولادَكُم عَطايا جَيِّدة، فكَم بِالأَولى أبوكُمُ الذي في السَّماواتِ يُعطي الخَيراتِ لِلذينَ يَسألونَه!

verses.syr: ܟܠ ܡܐ ܕܨܒܝܢ ܐܢܬܘܢ ܕܢܥܒܕܘܢ ܠܟܘܢ ܒܢܝ ܐܢܫܐ ܗܟܢܐ ܐܦ ܐܢܬܘܢ ܥܒܕܘ ܠܗܘܢ ܗܢܘ ܓܝܪ ܢܡܘܣܐ ܘܢܒܝܐ
verses.el: Πάντα οὖν ὅσα ἐὰν θέλητε ἵνα ποιῶσιν ὑμῖν οἱ ἄνθρωποι, οὕτως καὶ ὑμεῖς ποιεῖτε αὐτοῖς· οὗτος γάρ ἐστιν ὁ νόμος καὶ οἱ προφῆται.
verses.en: All that you wish the sons of men to do for you, thus also do for them; for this is the Law and the Prophets.
verses.ar: كُلُّ ما تُريدونَ أن يَصنَعَ لَكُم بَنو البَشَر، كذلكَ اصنَعوا أنتُم لَهُم؛ فهذا هو النّاموسُ والأنبياء.

verses.syr: ܥܘܠܘ ܒܬܪܥܐ ܐܠܝܨܐ ܕܦܬܐ ܗܘ ܬܪܥܐ ܘܐܪܘܝܚܐ ܐܘܪܚܐ ܐܝܕܐ ܕܡܘܒܠܐ ܠܐܒܕܢܐ ܘܣܓܝܐܐ ܐܢܘܢ ܐܝܠܝܢ ܕܐܙܠܝܢ ܒܗ
verses.el: Εἰσέλθατε διὰ τῆς στενῆς πύλης· ὅτι πλατεῖα ἡ πύλη καὶ εὐρύχωρος ἡ ὁδὸς ἡ ἀπάγουσα εἰς τὴν ἀπώλειαν, καὶ πολλοί εἰσιν οἱ εἰσερχόμενοι δι’ αὐτῆς·
verses.en: Enter by the narrow gate; for wide is the gate and broad is the way that leads to destruction, and many are those who go by it.
verses.ar: ادخُلوا مِن البابِ الضَّيِّق؛ لأنَّ البابَ واسِعٌ والطَّريقَ رَحبٌ الذي يَؤُولُ إلى الهَلاك، وكثيرونَ هُمُ الذينَ يَسلُكونَه.

verses.syr: ܡܐ ܩܛܝܢ ܬܪܥܐ ܘܐܠܝܨܐ ܐܘܪܚܐ ܕܡܘܒܠܐ ܠܚܝܐ ܘܙܥܘܪܐ ܐܢܘܢ ܐܝܠܝܢ ܕܡܫܟܚܝܢ ܠܗ
verses.el: ὅτι στενὴ ἡ πύλη καὶ τεθλιμμένη ἡ ὁδὸς ἡ ἀπάγουσα εἰς τὴν ζωήν, καὶ ὀλίγοι εἰσὶν οἱ εὑρίσκοντες αὐτήν.
verses.en: How narrow is the gate and pressed is the way that leads to life, and few are those who find it!
verses.ar: ما أضيَقَ البابَ وأضيَقَ الطَّريقَ الذي يَؤُولُ إلى الحَياة، وقَليلونَ هُمُ الذينَ يَجِدونَه!

verses.syr: ܐܙܕܗܪܘ ܡܢ ܢܒܝܐ ܕܓܠܐ ܕܐܬܝܢ ܠܘܬܟܘܢ ܒܠܒܘܫܐ ܕܐܡܪܐ ܡܢ ܠܓܘ ܕܝܢ ܐܝܬܝܗܘܢ ܕܐܒܐ ܚܛܘܦܐ
verses.el: Προσέχετε ἀπὸ τῶν ψευδοπροφητῶν, οἵτινες ἔρχονται πρὸς ὑμᾶς ἐν ἐνδύμασιν προβάτων ἔσωθεν δέ εἰσιν λύκοι ἅρπαγες.
verses.en: Beware of false prophets, who come to you in the clothing of lambs, but from within they are ravening wolves.
verses.ar: احتَرِزوا مِن الأنبياءِ الكَذَبة، الذينَ يَأتونَكُم بِلِباسِ الحُملان، وهُم مِن داخِلٍ ذِئابٌ خاطِفة.

verses.syr: ܡܢ ܦܐܪܝܗܘܢ ܕܝܢ ܬܕܥܘܢ ܐܢܘܢ ܠܡܐ ܠܩܛܝܢ ܡܢ ܟܘܒܐ ܥܢܒܐ ܐܘ ܡܢ ܩܘܪܛܒܐ ܬܐܢܐ
verses.el: ἀπὸ τῶν καρπῶν αὐτῶν ἐπιγνώσεσθε αὐτούς. μήτι συλλέγουσιν ἀπὸ ἀκανθῶν σταφυλὰς ἢ ἀπὸ τριβόλων σῦκα;
verses.en: By their fruits you shall know them. Do they gather grapes from thorns, or figs from thistles?
verses.ar: مِن ثِمارِهِم تَعرِفونَهُم. هَل يَجتَنونَ مِن الشَّوكِ عِنَباً، أو مِن الحَسَكِ تيناً؟

verses.syr: ܗܟܢܐ ܟܠ ܐܝܠܢܐ ܛܒܐ ܦܐܪܐ ܫܦܝܪܐ ܥܒܕ ܐܝܠܢܐ ܕܝܢ ܒܝܫܐ ܦܐܪܐ ܒܝܫܐ ܥܒܕ
verses.el: οὕτως πᾶν δένδρον ἀγαθὸν καρποὺς καλοὺς ποιεῖ, τὸ δὲ σαπρὸν δένδρον καρποὺς πονηροὺς ποιεῖ·
verses.en: Thus every good tree bears good fruit, but an evil tree bears evil fruit.
verses.ar: كذلكَ كُلُّ شَجَرةٍ جَيِّدةٍ تُثمِرُ ثِماراً جَيِّدة، وأمّا الشَّجَرةُ الرَّديئةُ فتُثمِرُ ثِماراً رَديئة.

verses.syr: ܠܐ ܡܫܟܚ ܐܝܠܢܐ ܛܒܐ ܦܐܪܐ ܒܝܫܐ ܠܡܥܒܕ ܘܠܐ ܐܝܠܢܐ ܒܝܫܐ ܦܐܪܐ ܛܒܐ ܠܡܥܒܕ
verses.el: οὐ δύναται δένδρον ἀγαθὸν καρποὺς πονηροὺς ἐνεγκεῖν, οὐδὲ δένδρον σαπρὸν καρποὺς καλοὺς ἐνεγκεῖν.
verses.en: A good tree cannot bear evil fruit, nor an evil tree bear good fruit.
verses.ar: لا تَقدِرُ شَجَرةٌ جَيِّدةٌ أن تُثمِرَ ثِماراً رَديئة، ولا شَجَرةٌ رَديئةٌ أن تُثمِرَ ثِماراً جَيِّدة.

verses.syr: ܟܠ ܐܝܠܢܐ ܕܠܐ ܥܒܕ ܦܐܪܐ ܛܒܐ ܡܬܦܣܩ ܘܒܢܘܪܐ ܢܦܠ
verses.el: πᾶν δένδρον μὴ ποιοῦν καρπὸν καλὸν ἐκκόπτεται καὶ εἰς πῦρ βάλλεται.
verses.en: Every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and falls into the fire.
verses.ar: كُلُّ شَجَرةٍ لا تُثمِرُ ثَمَراً جَيِّداً تُقطَعُ وتُطرَحُ في النار.

verses.syr: ܡܕܝܢ ܡܢ ܦܐܪܝܗܘܢ ܬܕܥܘܢ ܐܢܘܢ
verses.el: ἄραγε ἀπὸ τῶν καρπῶν αὐτῶν ἐπιγνώσεσθε αὐτούς.
verses.en: Therefore by their fruits you shall know them.
verses.ar: فمِن ثِمارِهِم تَعرِفونَهُم.

verses.syr: ܠܐ ܗܘܐ ܟܠ ܕܐܡܪ ܠܝ ܡܪܝ ܡܪܝ ܥܐܠ ܠܡܠܟܘܬܐ ܕܫܡܝܐ ܐܠܐ ܡܢ ܕܥܒܕ ܨܒܝܢܗ ܕܐܒܝ ܕܒܫܡܝܐ
verses.el: Οὐ πᾶς ὁ λέγων μοι Κύριε Κύριε, εἰσελεύσεται εἰς τὴν βασιλείαν τῶν οὐρανῶν, ἀλλ’ ὁ ποιῶν τὸ θέλημα τοῦ Πατρός μου τοῦ ἐν τοῖς οὐρανοῖς.
verses.en: Not every one who says to me "My Lord, my Lord" shall enter the sovereignty of the heavens, but he who does the will of my Father who is in the heavens.
verses.ar: لَيسَ كُلُّ مَن يَقولُ لي «يا رَبِّ يا رَبّ» يَدخُلُ مَلَكوتَ السَّماوات، بل مَن يَعمَلُ بِمَشيئةِ أبي الذي في السَّماوات.

verses.syr: ܣܓܝܐܐ ܢܐܡܪܘܢ ܠܝ ܒܗܘ ܝܘܡܐ ܡܪܝ ܡܪܝ ܠܐ ܒܫܡܟ ܐܬܢܒܝܢ ܘܒܫܡܟ ܫܐܕܐ ܐܦܩܢ ܘܒܫܡܟ ܚܝܠܐ ܣܓܝܐܐ ܥܒܕܢ
verses.el: πολλοὶ ἐροῦσίν μοι ἐν ἐκείνῃ τῇ ἡμέρᾳ Κύριε Κύριε, οὐ τῷ σῷ ὀνόματι ἐπροφητεύσαμεν, καὶ τῷ σῷ ὀνόματι δαιμόνια ἐξεβάλομεν, καὶ τῷ σῷ ὀνόματι δυνάμεις πολλὰς ἐποιήσαμεν;
verses.en: Many shall say to me on that day: "My Lord, my Lord — did we not prophesy in your name, and in your name cast out demons, and in your name do many mighty works?"
verses.ar: كثيرونَ يَقولونَ لي في ذلكَ اليَوم: «يا رَبِّ يا رَبّ — أوَلَم نَتَنَبَّأ بِاسمِك، وبِاسمِكَ نُخرِج الشَّياطين، وبِاسمِكَ نَصنَع مُعجِزاتٍ كثيرة؟»

verses.syr: ܘܗܝܕܝܢ ܐܘܕܐ ܠܗܘܢ ܕܡܢ ܡܬܘܡ ܠܐ ܝܕܥܬܟܘܢ ܐܪܚܩܘ ܠܟܘܢ ܡܢܝ ܦܠܚܝ ܥܘܠܐ
verses.el: καὶ τότε ὁμολογήσω αὐτοῖς ὅτι Οὐδέποτε ἔγνων ὑμᾶς· ἀποχωρεῖτε ἀπ’ ἐμοῦ οἱ ἐργαζόμενοι τὴν ἀνομίαν.
verses.en: And then I shall declare to them: "I never knew you. Depart from me, workers of wrongness."
verses.ar: وحينَئذٍ أُصَرِّحُ لَهُم: «لَم أعرِفكُم قَطّ. ابتَعِدوا عَنِّي يا فاعِلي الإثم.»

verses.syr: ܟܠ ܗܟܝܠ ܕܫܡܥ ܡܠܝ ܗܠܝܢ ܘܥܒܕ ܠܗܝܢ ܢܬܕܡܐ ܠܓܒܪܐ ܚܟܝܡܐ ܗܘ ܕܒܢܐ ܒܝܬܗ ܥܠ ܫܘܥܐ
verses.el: Πᾶς οὖν ὅστις ἀκούει μου τοὺς λόγους τούτους καὶ ποιεῖ αὐτούς, ὁμοιωθήσεται ἀνδρὶ φρονίμῳ, ὅστις ᾠκοδόμησεν αὐτοῦ τὴν οἰκίαν ἐπὶ τὴν πέτραν.
verses.en: Every one therefore who hears these words of mine and does them shall be likened to a wise man who built his house upon the rock.
verses.ar: فكُلُّ مَن يَسمَعُ هذهِ الكَلاماتِ مِنِّي ويَعمَلُ بِها يُشَبَّهُ بِرَجُلٍ عاقِلٍ بَنى بَيتَهُ على الصَّخرة.

verses.syr: ܘܢܚܬ ܡܛܪܐ ܘܐܬܘ ܢܗܪܘܬܐ ܘܢܫܒ ܪܘܚܐ ܘܐܬܛܪܝܘ ܒܗ ܒܒܝܬܐ ܗܘ ܘܠܐ ܢܦܠ ܫܬܐܣܘܗܝ ܓܝܪ ܥܠ ܫܘܥܐ ܣܝܡܢ ܗܘܝ
verses.el: καὶ κατέβη ἡ βροχὴ καὶ ἦλθον οἱ ποταμοὶ καὶ ἔπνευσαν οἱ ἄνεμοι καὶ προσέπεσαν τῇ οἰκίᾳ ἐκείνῃ, καὶ οὐκ ἔπεσεν· τεθεμελίωτο γὰρ ἐπὶ τὴν πέτραν.
verses.en: And the rain descended, and the rivers came, and the wind blew and beat upon that house — and it did not fall, for its foundations were set upon rock.
verses.ar: فَنَزَلَ المَطَرُ وجاءَت السُّيولُ وهَبَّت الرِّياحُ وضَرَبَت ذلكَ البَيتَ — فلَم يَسقُط، لأنَّ أساسَهُ كانَ على الصَّخرة.

verses.syr: ܘܟܠ ܡܢ ܕܫܡܥ ܡܠܝ ܗܠܝܢ ܘܠܐ ܥܒܕ ܠܗܝܢ ܢܬܕܡܐ ܠܓܒܪܐ ܣܟܠܐ ܕܒܢܐ ܒܝܬܗ ܥܠ ܚܠܐ
verses.el: καὶ πᾶς ὁ ἀκούων μου τοὺς λόγους τούτους καὶ μὴ ποιῶν αὐτοὺς ὁμοιωθήσεται ἀνδρὶ μωρῷ, ὅστις ᾠκοδόμησεν αὐτοῦ τὴν οἰκίαν ἐπὶ τὴν ἄμμον.
verses.en: And every one who hears these words of mine and does not do them shall be likened to a foolish man who built his house upon the sand.
verses.ar: وكُلُّ مَن يَسمَعُ هذهِ الكَلاماتِ مِنِّي ولا يَعمَلُ بِها يُشَبَّهُ بِرَجُلٍ أحمَقَ بَنى بَيتَهُ على الرَّمل.

verses.syr: ܘܢܚܬ ܡܛܪܐ ܘܐܬܘ ܢܗܪܘܬܐ ܘܢܫܒ ܪܘܚܐ ܘܐܬܛܪܝܘ ܒܒܝܬܐ ܗܘ ܘܢܦܠ ܘܗܘܬ ܡܦܘܠܬܗ ܪܒܐ
verses.el: καὶ κατέβη ἡ βροχὴ καὶ ἦλθον οἱ ποταμοὶ καὶ ἔπνευσαν οἱ ἄνεμοι καὶ προσέκοψαν τῇ οἰκίᾳ ἐκείνῃ, καὶ ἔπεσεν, καὶ ἦν ἡ πτῶσις αὐτῆς μεγάλη.
verses.en: And the rain descended, and the rivers came, and the wind blew and beat upon that house — and it fell, and great was its fall.
verses.ar: فَنَزَلَ المَطَرُ وجاءَت السُّيولُ وهَبَّت الرِّياحُ وضَرَبَت ذلكَ البَيتَ — فَسَقَط، وكانَ سُقوطُهُ عَظيماً.

verses.syr: ܘܗܘܐ ܕܟܕ ܫܠܡ ܝܫܘܥ ܡܠܐ ܗܠܝܢ ܬܗܝܪܝܢ ܗܘܘ ܟܢܫܐ ܥܠ ܝܘܠܦܢܗ
verses.el: Καὶ ἐγένετο ὅτε ἐτέλεσεν ὁ Ἰησοῦς τοὺς λόγους τούτους, ἐξεπλήσσοντο οἱ ὄχλοι ἐπὶ τῇ διδαχῇ αὐτοῦ·
verses.en: And it came to pass, when Yeshua had finished these words, the crowds were amazed at his teaching.
verses.ar: وحِينَ فَرَغَ يَشوعُ مِن هذهِ الأقوال، بُهِتَ الجُموعُ مِن تَعليمِه.

verses.syr: ܡܠܦ ܗܘܐ ܠܗܘܢ ܓܝܪ ܐܝܟ ܡܫܠܛܐ ܘܠܐ ܐܝܟ ܣܦܪܝܗܘܢ ܘܦܪܝܫܐ
verses.el: ἦν γὰρ διδάσκων αὐτοὺς ὡς ἐξουσίαν ἔχων, καὶ οὐχ ὡς οἱ γραμματεῖς αὐτῶν.
verses.en: For he was teaching them as one having authority, and not as their scribes and Separated-ones.
verses.ar: لأنَّهُ كانَ يُعَلِّمُهُم كَمَن له سُلطان، لا كَكَتَبَتِهِم والفَرِّيسيِّين.

chapters.commentary:
# Annotations on Matthew Chapter 7 — Judging, Seeking, and the Two Foundations

## Syriac-First Approach

This chapter is translated primarily from the Syriac Peshitta, with Greek noted where the two diverge. The Peshitta preserves Aramaic — the language Jesus actually spoke. The Greek New Testament is already a translation; the Syriac may be closer to the original words.

## Root-Recovery Choices

### v.1: ܠܐ ܬܕܘܢܘܢ (la tedunun) — "Do not judge"
Root ܕ-ܝ-ܢ (d-y-n): to judge, to govern, to sentence. Cognate with Hebrew דין (din, "judgment/law") and Arabic دين (dīn, "religion/judgment/way of life"). The word ܕܝܢ is also the Aramaic/Arabic root that gives us the Qur'anic phrase يوم الدين (yawm al-dīn, "Day of Judgment/Religion"). The prohibition on judging is not absolute — v.5 assumes one can eventually see clearly — but it cautions against rendering verdicts on others when the judge's own vision is impaired.

### v.2: ܡܫܘܚܬܐ (mashuḥta) — "measure"
Root ܡ-ܫ-ḥ (m-sh-ḥ) — interestingly the same root as ܡܫܝܚܐ (the Anointed) from a different but related Semitic base. The law of reciprocal measure: by the same measure you use, it shall be measured back. Greek μέτρον (metron). This is a fundamental Semitic wisdom principle — "as you do, so it is done to you." Cognate with Hebrew מדה (midah, "measure/attribute") in the rabbinic principle of מידה כנגד מידה (measure for measure).

### v.5: ܢܣܒ ܒܐܦܐ (nasav b-afe) — "Face-taker!"
The same word from ch. 6 — "one who takes on a face/puts on an appearance." The charge here is pointed at the would-be critic: before correcting another, acknowledge your own pretense. The beam (ܩܪܝܬܐ, qariyta) in your own eye is the self-deception of the face-taker. Consistent with the Greek ὑποκριτά (hypokrita).

### v.6: ܡܐ ܕܩܕܝܫ (ma d-qaddish) — "what is holy"
Root ܩ-ܕ-ܫ (q-d-sh): holy, set apart. "Do not give what is holy to the dogs." ܩܕܝܫܐ (qaddisha, "holy/set apart") appears here in contrast to the profane. The teaching is about discernment of context — the same content offered to the wrong audience becomes profaned. Dogs and swine were considered ritually unclean in the Semitic context; the saying is about the mismatch between the holy and the unprepared.

### v.7: ܫܐܠܘ ... ܒܥܘ ... ܩܘܫܘ (sha'alu... beʿu... qushu) — "Ask... Seek... Knock"
Three ascending verbs of active petition. Root ܫ-ܐ-ܠ (sh-'-l): to ask, to inquire. Cognate with Hebrew שאל (sha'al, "to ask/request") — the same root as שאול (she'ol, "the unseen," literally "the place of asking"). Root ܒ-ܥ (b-ʿ): to seek, to desire, to look for. Cognate with Arabic بغى (baghā, "to seek") and Hebrew בקש (biqesh, "to seek"). Root ܩ-ܫ (q-sh) / ܕܩ (daq): to knock, to press. The triple imperative — ask, seek, knock — escalates from verbal request to active searching to physical insistence at a threshold. The passive responses ("shall be given," "shall find," "shall be opened") imply a divine Giver, a divine Finder, a divine Opener.

### v.11: ܡܘܗܒܬܐ ܛܒܬܐ (mawhabta ṭavta) — "good gifts"
Root ܡ-ܘ-ܗ-ܒ (m-w-h-b): to give, to bestow. ܡܘܗܒܬܐ (mawhabta) = gift, endowment. Cognate with Arabic وهب (wahaba, "to give freely") — as in عبد الوهاب (ʿAbd al-Wahhāb, "Servant of the Bestower"). The name Wahab/Wahib is one of the divine names in Islamic tradition. The argument is a fortiori: if you who are evil give good gifts, how much more God.

### v.12: ܟܠ ܡܕܡ ܕܨܒܝܢ (kol meddem d-tsevin) — "All that you wish"
This is the Golden Rule in Aramaic. Root ܨ-ܒ-ܝ (ts-b-y): to will, to desire, to choose. The same root as ܨܒܝܢܟ (tsevyonakh, "Your will/desire") from the Lord's Prayer in ch. 6 — God's wishing/desiring, and humanity's wishing/desiring, using the same root. The Golden Rule is grounded in this shared capacity for desire.

### v.13-14: ܬܪܥܐ ܐܠܝܨܐ ... ܐܘܪܚܐ ܐܠܝܨܬܐ (tarʿa 'alitsa... urḥa 'alitsa) — "narrow gate... pressed way"
Root ܐ-ܠ-ܝ-ܨ (a-l-y-ts): narrow, constricted, pressed. The Greek στενή (stenē, "narrow") is equivalent. The image of two ways — the broad road and the narrow path — is pervasive in Wisdom literature (cf. Psalm 1, Proverbs 4:18-19, Deuteronomy 30:19). The narrow path leads to ܚܝܐ (ḥayye, "life") — root ܚ-ܝ (ḥ-y): life, living. Cognate with Hebrew חיים (ḥayyim) and Arabic حياة (ḥayāh).

### v.21: ܡܪܝ ܡܪܝ (Mari, Mari) — "My Lord, my Lord"
Root ܡ-ܪ-ܝ (m-r-y): lord, master. ܡܪܝܐ (Marya) is the Syriac rendering of YHWH; ܡܪܝ (Mari) = "my Lord." This is the intimate address form. The doubling (ܡܪܝ ܡܪܝ) intensifies — it is not enough to invoke the name. The criterion for entering the sovereignty is not verbal invocation but doing the will of the Father. This parallels the Qur'anic warning against those who claim faith with their mouths while their hearts are elsewhere.

### v.24-27: ܫܘܫܦܐ (shushfa) — "rock"; ܚܠܐ (ḥala) — "sand"
The parable of the two builders. ܫܘܫܦܐ (shushfa) = rock, stone foundation. The Syriac uses ܫܘܫܦܐ while Greek uses πέτρα (petra). The same word Kepha (Cephas = Peter = Rock) that names Shimon is from the related Aramaic root ܟܐܦܐ (kepha, "rock"). The house on rock withstands rain, rivers, and wind; the house on sand collapses. The contrast is between hearing-and-doing versus hearing-only.

### v.28-29: ܫܘܠܛܢܐ (shultana) — "authority"
Root ܫ-ܠ-ܛ (sh-l-ṭ): to rule, to have authority, to govern. ܫܘܠܛܢܐ (shultana) = authority, dominion. Cognate with Arabic سلطان (sulṭān, "sultan/authority/power"). The crowds were amazed because the Anointed taught "as one having authority" — not appealing to the chain of tradition but speaking from the authority of the sovereignty itself. This authority (shultana) is distinct from but related to ܡܠܟܘܬܐ (malkuta, "sovereignty") — it is the executive dimension of divine rule.

## Cross-Scriptural Root Connections

| Syriac | Hebrew | Arabic | Shared meaning | Qur'an connection |
|--------|--------|--------|---------------|-------------------|
| ܕܝܢ (din) | דין (din) | دين (dīn) | judgment/religion | 1:4 — يوم الدين |
| ܫܠܛ (shalat) | שלט (shalit) | سلطان (sulṭān) | authority/power | 2:255 — ولا يحيطون بشيء من علمه |
| ܚܝ (ḥay) | חיים (ḥayyim) | حياة (ḥayāh) | life/living | 2:255 — الحي القيوم |
| ܡܘܗܒ (mawhab) | מתנה (matanah) | وهاب (wahhāb) | gift/bestowing | Throughout — الوهاب |
| ܩܕܫ (qaddash) | קדש (qodesh) | قدس (quds) | holy/set apart | Throughout |
| ܫܠܡ (shlam) | שלם (shalem) | سلام (salām) | wholeness/peace | Throughout |

## Bahá'í Interpretive Notes

The teaching "by their fruits you shall know them" (v.16-20) is a cornerstone of Bahá'í reasoning about the validity of religious claims. 'Abdu'l-Bahá repeatedly appeals to this principle: the tree of revelation is known by its fruits — the transformation of individual character and social reality. The two builders (v.24-27) reflect the Bahá'í distinction between the "Word of God" as a foundation versus mere profession of faith. Bahá'u'lláh in the Kitáb-i-Íqán speaks extensively of the need to follow the inner meaning of scriptures (doing) rather than clinging to literal forms (merely hearing). The narrow gate and the wide gate echo the Bahá'í teaching that in every age, the path of the new Manifestation is followed by few while the comfortable path of convention is followed by many.

## Integrative Links

- **v.1-2 "Do not judge" ↔ Qur'an 49:11-12**: يا أيها الذين آمنوا لا يسخر قوم من قوم — "O you who trust, let not a people mock another people." The same prohibition on rendering verdicts from a position of presumed superiority.
- **v.7-8 "Ask, seek, knock" ↔ Qur'an 2:186**: ادعوني أستجب لكم — "Call upon Me, and I will answer you." Both teach the responsiveness of God to earnest seeking.
- **v.12 Golden Rule ↔ Leviticus 19:18**: ואהבת לרעך כמוך — "Love your neighbor as yourself." The Anointed summarizes this as "the Law and the Prophets" — rooting the Golden Rule directly in Torah.
- **v.13-14 two ways ↔ Psalm 1**: The two ways of the psalm — the way of the righteous and the way of the wicked — are restated here as the narrow and broad paths.
- **v.24 building on rock ↔ Isaiah 28:16**: "Behold I am laying in Zion a foundation stone, a tested stone, a precious cornerstone, a sure foundation." The "rock" foundation has its roots in the prophetic tradition.

chapters.title: Matthew — ܩܦܠܐܘܢ 8
chapters.subtitle: Matthew — Chapter 8

verses.syr: ܟܕ ܢܚܬ ܕܝܢ ܡܢ ܛܘܪܐ ܢܩܦܘܗܝ ܟܢܫܐ ܣܓܝܐܐ
verses.el: Καταβάντος δὲ αὐτοῦ ἀπὸ τοῦ ὄρους ἠκολούθησαν αὐτῷ ὄχλοι πολλοί.
verses.en: And when he came down from the mountain, great crowds followed him.
verses.ar: ولمّا نَزَلَ مِن الجَبَل تَبِعَتهُ جُموعٌ كثيرة.

verses.syr: ܘܗܐ ܓܪܒܐ ܚܕ ܐܬܐ ܣܓܕ ܠܗ ܘܐܡܪ ܡܪܝ ܐܢ ܨܒܐ ܐܢܬ ܡܫܟܚ ܐܢܬ ܠܡܕܟܝܘܬܝ
verses.el: καὶ ἰδοὺ λεπρὸς προσελθὼν προσεκύνει αὐτῷ λέγων Κύριε, ἐὰν θέλῃς δύνασαί με καθαρίσαι.
verses.en: And behold, a leper came and bowed down to him and said: My Lord, if you will, you are able to cleanse me.
verses.ar: وإذا أبرَصُ جاءَ وسَجَدَ له وقال: يا رَبِّ، إن أَرَدتَ تَقدِرُ أن تُطَهِّرَني.

verses.syr: ܘܦܫܛ ܐܝܕܗ ܝܫܘܥ ܩܪܒ ܠܗ ܘܐܡܪ ܨܒܐ ܐܢܐ ܐܬܕܟܐ ܘܒܗ ܒܫܥܬܐ ܐܬܕܟܝ ܓܪܒܗ
verses.el: καὶ ἐκτείνας τὴν χεῖρα ἥψατο αὐτοῦ λέγων Θέλω, καθαρίσθητι. καὶ εὐθέως ἐκαθερίσθη αὐτοῦ ἡ λέπρα.
verses.en: And Yeshua stretched out his hand and drew near to him and said: I will; be cleansed. And in that very hour his leprosy was cleansed.
verses.ar: فمَدَّ يَشوعُ يَدَهُ ولَمَسَهُ وقال: أُريدُ فَاطهُر. وفي تِلكَ السّاعةِ طَهُرَ بَرَصُه.

verses.syr: ܘܐܡܪ ܠܗ ܝܫܘܥ ܚܙܝ ܠܡܐ ܠܐܢܫ ܐܡܪ ܐܢܬ ܐܠܐ ܙܠ ܚܘܐ ܢܦܫܟ ܠܟܗܢܐ ܘܩܪܒ ܩܘܪܒܢܐ ܐܝܟ ܕܦܩܕ ܡܘܫܐ ܠܣܗܕܘܬܗܘܢ
verses.el: καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ ὁ Ἰησοῦς Ὅρα μηδενὶ εἴπῃς, ἀλλὰ ὕπαγε σεαυτὸν δεῖξον τῷ ἱερεῖ, καὶ προσένεγκον τὸ δῶρον ὃ προσέταξεν Μωϋσῆς εἰς μαρτύριον αὐτοῖς.
verses.en: And Yeshua said to him: See that you tell no man, but go, show yourself to the priest, and offer the offering as Mosheh commanded, for a witness unto them.
verses.ar: وقالَ له يَشوع: إيّاكَ أن تُخبِرَ أحَداً، بل اذهَب أرِ نَفسَكَ لِلكاهِن، وقَدِّم القُربانَ الذي أمَرَ بهِ موسى، شَهادةً لَهُم.

verses.syr: ܟܕ ܥܠ ܕܝܢ ܝܫܘܥ ܠܟܦܪܢܚܘܡ ܩܪܒ ܠܗ ܩܢܛܪܘܢܐ ܚܕ ܘܒܥܐ ܗܘܐ ܡܢܗ
verses.el: Εἰσελθόντος δὲ αὐτοῦ εἰς Καφαρναοὺμ προσῆλθεν αὐτῷ ἑκατόνταρχος παρακαλῶν αὐτὸν
verses.en: And when Yeshua entered Kfar Naḥum, a centurion drew near to him and was beseeching him,
verses.ar: ولمّا دَخَلَ يَشوعُ كَفَرناحوم، دَنا إليهِ قائِدُ مِئَةٍ يَتَوَسَّلُ إليه،

verses.syr: ܘܐܡܪ ܡܪܝ ܛܠܝܝ ܪܡܐ ܒܒܝܬܐ ܘܡܫܪܝ ܘܒܝܫܐܝܬ ܡܫܬܢܩ
verses.el: καὶ λέγων Κύριε, ὁ παῖς μου βέβληται ἐν τῇ οἰκίᾳ παραλυτικός, δεινῶς βασανιζόμενος.
verses.en: and said: My Lord, my boy lies in the house, paralyzed and grievously tormented.
verses.ar: وقال: يا رَبِّ، غُلامي مُضطَجِعٌ في البَيتِ مَفلوجٌ يَتَعَذَّبُ عَذاباً شَديداً.

verses.syr: ܐܡܪ ܠܗ ܝܫܘܥ ܐܢܐ ܐܬܐ ܘܐܣܝܘܗܝ
verses.el: λέγει αὐτῷ Ἐγὼ ἐλθὼν θεραπεύσω αὐτόν.
verses.en: Yeshua said to him: I will come and heal him.
verses.ar: قالَ له يَشوع: أنا آتي وأَشفيه.

verses.syr: ܥܢܐ ܩܢܛܪܘܢܐ ܗܘ ܘܐܡܪ ܡܪܝ ܠܐ ܫܘܐ ܐܢܐ ܕܬܥܘܠ ܬܚܝܬ ܡܛܠܠܝ ܐܠܐ ܒܠܚܘܕ ܐܡܪ ܒܡܠܬܐ ܘܢܬܐܣܐ ܛܠܝܝ
verses.el: ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ ὁ ἑκατόνταρχος ἔφη Κύριε, οὐκ εἰμὶ ἱκανὸς ἵνα μου ὑπὸ τὴν στέγην εἰσέλθῃς· ἀλλὰ μόνον εἰπὲ λόγῳ, καὶ ἰαθήσεται ὁ παῖς μου.
verses.en: The centurion answered and said: My Lord, I am not worthy that you should come under my roof; but only speak the word, and my boy shall be healed.
verses.ar: أجابَ قائِدُ المِئَةِ وقال: يا رَبِّ، لَستُ مُستَحِقًّا أن تَدخُلَ تَحتَ سَقفي؛ لكِن قُل كَلِمةً فقَط فيَشفى غُلامي.

verses.syr: ܐܦ ܐܢܐ ܓܝܪ ܓܒܪܐ ܐܢܐ ܕܬܚܝܬ ܫܘܠܛܢܐ ܘܐܝܬ ܬܚܝܬ ܐܝܕܝ ܐܣܛܪܛܝܘܛܐ ܘܐܡܪ ܐܢܐ ܠܗܢܐ ܕܙܠ ܘܐܙܠ ܘܠܐܚܪܢܐ ܕܬܐ ܘܐܬܐ ܘܠܥܒܕܝ ܕܥܒܕ ܗܕܐ ܘܥܒܕ
verses.el: καὶ γὰρ ἐγὼ ἄνθρωπός εἰμι ὑπὸ ἐξουσίαν, ἔχων ὑπ’ ἐμαυτὸν στρατιώτας, καὶ λέγω τούτῳ Πορεύθητι, καὶ πορεύεται, καὶ ἄλλῳ Ἔρχου, καὶ ἔρχεται, καὶ τῷ δούλῳ μου Ποίησον τοῦτο, καὶ ποιεῖ.
verses.en: For I too am a man under authority, and I have soldiers under my hand, and I say to this one "Go!" and he goes, and to another "Come!" and he comes, and to my servant "Do this!" and he does it.
verses.ar: فإنِّي أيضاً رَجُلٌ تَحتَ سُلطانٍ وتَحتَ يَدي جُندٌ، فأقولُ لِهذا «اذهَب» فيَذهَب، ولِآخَرَ «تعال» فيَأتي، ولِعَبدي «اصنَع هذا» فيَصنَعُه.

verses.syr: ܟܕ ܫܡܥ ܕܝܢ ܝܫܘܥ ܐܬܕܡܪ ܘܐܡܪ ܠܕܐܬܝܢ ܥܡܗ ܐܡܝܢ ܐܡܪ ܐܢܐ ܠܟܘܢ ܕܐܦ ܠܐ ܒܐܝܣܪܐܝܠ ܐܫܟܚܬ ܐܝܟ ܗܕܐ ܗܝܡܢܘܬܐ
verses.el: ἀκούσας δὲ ὁ Ἰησοῦς ἐθαύμασεν καὶ εἶπεν τοῖς ἀκολουθοῦσιν Ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν, παρ’ οὐδενὶ τοσαύτην πίστιν ἐν τῷ Ἰσραὴλ εὗρον.
verses.en: And when Yeshua heard, he marveled and said to those who came with him: Amen I say to you, not even in Yisrael have I found trust like this.
verses.ar: فلمّا سَمِعَ يَشوعُ تَعَجَّبَ وقالَ لِلذينَ كانوا مَعَهُ: الحَقَّ أقولُ لَكُم: لَم أجِد في يِسرائيلَ إيماناً مِثلَ هذا.

verses.syr: ܐܡܪ ܐܢܐ ܠܟܘܢ ܕܝܢ ܕܣܓܝܐܐ ܢܐܬܘܢ ܡܢ ܡܕܢܚܐ ܘܡܢ ܡܥܪܒܐ ܘܢܣܬܡܟܘܢ ܥܡ ܐܒܪܗܡ ܘܐܝܣܚܩ ܘܝܥܩܘܒ ܒܡܠܟܘܬܐ ܕܫܡܝܐ
verses.el: λέγω δὲ ὑμῖν ὅτι πολλοὶ ἀπὸ ἀνατολῶν καὶ δυσμῶν ἥξουσιν καὶ ἀνακλιθήσονται μετὰ Ἀβραὰμ καὶ Ἰσαὰκ καὶ Ἰακὼβ ἐν τῇ βασιλείᾳ τῶν οὐρανῶν·
verses.en: But I say to you that many shall come from the east and from the west and shall recline with Avraham and Yitsḥaq and Yaʿaqov in the sovereignty of the heavens.
verses.ar: لكِنِّي أقولُ لَكُم: إنَّ كثيرينَ يَأتونَ مِن المَشرِقِ والمَغرِب، ويَتَّكِئونَ مَعَ إبراهيمَ وإسحاقَ ويعقوبَ في مَلَكوتِ السَّماوات.

verses.syr: ܒܢܝܗ ܕܝܢ ܕܡܠܟܘܬܐ ܢܦܩܘܢ ܠܚܫܘܟܐ ܒܪܝܐ ܬܡܢ ܢܗܘܐ ܒܟܝܐ ܘܚܘܪܩ ܫܢܐ
verses.el: οἱ δὲ υἱοὶ τῆς βασιλείας ἐκβληθήσονται εἰς τὸ σκότος τὸ ἐξώτερον· ἐκεῖ ἔσται ὁ κλαυθμὸς καὶ ὁ βρυγμὸς τῶν ὀδόντων.
verses.en: But the sons of the sovereignty shall be cast out into the outer darkness; there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
verses.ar: أمّا أبناءُ المَلَكوتِ فيُطرَحونَ في الظُّلمةِ الخارِجة؛ هُناكَ يَكونُ البُكاءُ وصَريرُ الأسنان.

verses.syr: ܘܐܡܪ ܝܫܘܥ ܠܩܢܛܪܘܢܐ ܗܘ ܙܠ ܐܝܟܢܐ ܕܗܝܡܢܬ ܢܗܘܐ ܠܟ ܘܐܬܐܣܝ ܛܠܝܗ ܒܗ ܒܫܥܬܐ
verses.el: καὶ εἶπεν ὁ Ἰησοῦς τῷ ἑκατοντάρχῃ Ὕπαγε, ὡς ἐπίστευσας γενηθήτω σοι. καὶ ἰάθη ὁ παῖς ἐν τῇ ὥρᾳ ἐκείνῃ.
verses.en: And Yeshua said to that centurion: Go; as you have trusted, let it be unto you. And his boy was healed in that very hour.
verses.ar: وقالَ يَشوعُ لِقائِدِ المِئَة: اذهَب، وكما آمَنتَ فَليَكُن لَك. وشُفِيَ غُلامُهُ في تِلكَ السّاعة.

verses.syr: ܘܐܬܐ ܝܫܘܥ ܠܒܝܬܗ ܕܫܡܥܘܢ ܘܚܙܐ ܠܚܡܬܗ ܕܪܡܝܐ ܘܐܚܝܕܐ ܠܗ ܐܫܬܐ
verses.el: Καὶ ἐλθὼν ὁ Ἰησοῦς εἰς τὴν οἰκίαν Πέτρου εἶδεν τὴν πενθερὰν αὐτοῦ βεβλημένην καὶ πυρέσσουσαν·
verses.en: And Yeshua came to the house of Shimon and saw his mother-in-law lying down, seized by fever.
verses.ar: وجاءَ يَشوعُ إلى بَيتِ شَمعونَ ورَأى حَماتَهُ مُضطَجِعةً مَحمومة.

verses.syr: ܘܩܪܒ ܠܐܝܕܗ ܘܫܒܩܬܗ ܐܫܬܐ ܘܩܡܬ ܘܡܫܡܫܐ ܗܘܬ ܠܗ
verses.el: καὶ ἥψατο τῆς χειρὸς αὐτῆς, καὶ ἀφῆκεν αὐτὴν ὁ πυρετός· καὶ ἠγέρθη, καὶ διηκόνει αὐτῷ.
verses.en: And he drew near to her hand, and the fever left her, and she arose and was serving him.
verses.ar: فلَمَسَ يَدَها فتَرَكَتها الحُمَّى، فقامَت وكانَت تَخدُمُه.

verses.syr: ܟܕ ܗܘܐ ܕܝܢ ܪܡܫܐ ܩܪܒܘ ܩܕܡܘܗܝ ܕܝܘܢܐ ܣܓܝܐܐ ܘܐܦܩ ܕܝܘܝܗܘܢ ܒܡܠܬܐ ܘܠܟܠܗܘܢ ܐܝܠܝܢ ܕܒܝܫܐܝܬ ܥܒܝܕܝܢ ܗܘܘ ܐܣܝ ܐܢܘܢ
verses.el: Ὀψίας δὲ γενομένης προσήνεγκαν αὐτῷ δαιμονιζομένους πολλούς· καὶ ἐξέβαλεν τὰ πνεύματα λόγῳ, καὶ πάντας τοὺς κακῶς ἔχοντας ἐθεράπευσεν·
verses.en: And when evening came, they brought before him many who had demons, and he cast out their demons by the word, and all those who were badly afflicted he healed —
verses.ar: وعِندَ المَساءِ قَدَّموا إليهِ كثيرينَ مِمَّن بِهِم شَياطين، فأخرَجَ الشَّياطينَ بِالكَلِمة، وشَفى جميعَ المُصابين —

verses.syr: ܐܝܟ ܕܢܬܡܠܐ ܡܕܡ ܕܐܬܐܡܪ ܒܝܕ ܐܫܥܝܐ ܢܒܝܐ ܕܐܡܪ ܕܗܘ ܢܣܒ ܟܐܒܝܢ ܘܟܘܪܗܢܝܢ ܢܛܥܢ
verses.el: ὅπως πληρωθῇ τὸ ῥηθὲν διὰ Ἠσαΐου τοῦ προφήτου λέγοντος Αὐτὸς τὰς ἀσθενείας ἡμῶν ἔλαβεν καὶ τὰς νόσους ἐβάστασεν.
verses.en: so that what was spoken through Yeshayahu the prophet might be fulfilled, who said: He himself took our pains and bore our sicknesses.
verses.ar: لِيَتِمَّ ما قِيلَ بِيَدِ إشعياءَ النبيّ القائل: هو أخَذَ أوجاعَنا وحَمَلَ أمراضَنا.

verses.syr: ܟܕ ܚܙܐ ܕܝܢ ܝܫܘܥ ܟܢܫܐ ܣܓܝܐܐ ܕܚܕܝܪܝܢ ܠܗ ܦܩܕ ܕܢܐܙܠܘܢ ܠܥܒܪܐ
verses.el: Ἰδὼν δὲ ὁ Ἰησοῦς ὄχλον περὶ αὐτὸν ἐκέλευσεν ἀπελθεῖν εἰς τὸ πέραν.
verses.en: And when Yeshua saw a great crowd surrounding him, he commanded them to go to the other side.
verses.ar: ولمّا رَأى يَشوعُ جُموعاً كثيرةً حَولَهُ، أمَرَ بِالانصِرافِ إلى الجانِبِ الآخَر.

verses.syr: ܘܩܪܒ ܣܦܪܐ ܚܕ ܘܐܡܪ ܠܗ ܪܒܝ ܐܬܐ ܒܬܪܟ ܠܐܬܪ ܕܐܙܠ ܐܢܬ
verses.el: Καὶ προσελθὼν εἷς γραμματεὺς εἶπεν αὐτῷ Διδάσκαλε, ἀκολουθήσω σοι ὅπου ἐὰν ἀπέρχῃ.
verses.en: And a scribe came near and said to him: Rabbi, I will come after you wherever you go.
verses.ar: فدَنا كاتِبٌ وقالَ له: يا مُعَلِّم، أتبَعُكَ أيَّنَما ذَهَبتَ.

verses.syr: ܐܡܪ ܠܗ ܝܫܘܥ ܠܬܥܠܐ ܢܩܥܐ ܐܝܬ ܠܗܘܢ ܘܠܦܪܚܬܐ ܕܫܡܝܐ ܡܛܠܠܐ ܒܪܗ ܕܝܢ ܕܐܢܫܐ ܠܝܬ ܠܗ ܐܝܟܐ ܕܢܣܡܘܟ ܪܫܗ
verses.el: καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ ὁ Ἰησοῦς Αἱ ἀλώπεκες φωλεοὺς ἔχουσιν καὶ τὰ πετεινὰ τοῦ οὐρανοῦ κατασκηνώσεις, ὁ δὲ Υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου οὐκ ἔχει ποῦ τὴν κεφαλὴν κλίνῃ.
verses.en: Yeshua said to him: The foxes have dens, and the birds of the heavens have shelters, but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay his head.
verses.ar: قالَ له يَشوع: لِلثَّعالِبِ أوجِرةٌ ولِطُيورِ السَّماءِ أعشاش، وأمّا ابنُ الإنسانِ فلَيسَ له أينَ يَضَعُ رَأسَه.

verses.syr: ܐܚܪܢܐ ܕܝܢ ܡܢ ܬܠܡܝܕܘܗܝ ܐܡܪ ܠܗ ܡܪܝ ܐܦܣ ܠܝ ܠܘܩܕܡ ܐܙܠ ܐܩܒܘܪ ܐܒܝ
verses.el: ἕτερος δὲ τῶν μαθητῶν εἶπεν αὐτῷ Κύριε, ἐπίτρεψόν μοι πρῶτον ἀπελθεῖν καὶ θάψαι τὸν πατέρα μου.
verses.en: And another of his disciples said to him: My Lord, permit me first to go and bury my father.
verses.ar: وقالَ له آخَرُ مِن تَلاميذِه: يا رَبِّ، ائذَن لي أن أذهَبَ أوَّلاً وأدفِنَ أبي.

verses.syr: ܝܫܘܥ ܕܝܢ ܐܡܪ ܠܗ ܬܐ ܒܬܪܝ ܘܫܒܘܩ ܠܡܝܬܐ ܩܒܪܝܢ ܡܝܬܝܗܘܢ
verses.el: ὁ δὲ Ἰησοῦς λέγει αὐτῷ Ἀκολούθει μοι, καὶ ἄφες τοὺς νεκροὺς θάψαι τοὺς ἑαυτῶν νεκρούς.
verses.en: But Yeshua said to him: Come after me, and leave the dead to bury their own dead.
verses.ar: فقالَ له يَشوع: اتبَعني ودَع الموتى يَدفِنونَ مَوتاهُم.

verses.syr: ܘܟܕ ܣܠܩ ܝܫܘܥ ܠܣܦܝܢܬܐ ܣܠܩܘ ܥܡܗ ܬܠܡܝܕܘܗܝ
verses.el: Καὶ ἐμβάντι αὐτῷ εἰς τὸ πλοῖον ἠκολούθησαν αὐτῷ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ.
verses.en: And when Yeshua went up into the boat, his disciples went up with him.
verses.ar: ولمّا صَعِدَ يَشوعُ إلى السَّفينةِ صَعِدَ مَعَهُ تَلاميذُه.

verses.syr: ܘܗܐ ܙܘܥܐ ܪܒܐ ܗܘܐ ܒܝܡܐ ܐܝܟܢܐ ܕܐܠܦܐ ܬܬܟܣܐ ܡܢ ܓܠܠܐ ܗܘ ܕܝܢ ܝܫܘܥ ܕܡܝܟ ܗܘܐ
verses.el: καὶ ἰδοὺ σεισμὸς μέγας ἐγένετο ἐν τῇ θαλάσσῃ, ὥστε τὸ πλοῖον καλύπτεσθαι ὑπὸ τῶν κυμάτων· αὐτὸς δὲ ἐκάθευδεν.
verses.en: And behold, a great shaking arose on the sea, so that the boat was covered by the waves; but Yeshua was sleeping.
verses.ar: وإذا هَيَجانٌ عَظيمٌ حَدَثَ في البَحر، حتّى تَغَطَّت السَّفينةُ بِالأمواج؛ وأمّا يَشوعُ فكانَ نائِماً.

verses.syr: ܘܩܪܒܘ ܬܠܡܝܕܘܗܝ ܐܥܝܪܘܗܝ ܘܐܡܪܝܢ ܠܗ ܡܪܢ ܦܨܢ ܐܒܕܝܢ ܚܢܢ
verses.el: καὶ προσελθόντες ἤγειραν αὐτὸν λέγοντες Κύριε, σῶσον, ἀπολλύμεθα.
verses.en: And his disciples came near and woke him and said to him: Our Lord, rescue us — we are perishing!
verses.ar: فدَنا إليهِ تَلاميذُهُ وأيقَظوهُ وقالوا له: يا رَبَّنا، نَجِّنا — هَلَكنا!

verses.syr: ܐܡܪ ܠܗܘܢ ܝܫܘܥ ܠܡܢܐ ܕܚܘܠܬܢܝܢ ܐܢܬܘܢ ܙܥܘܪܝ ܗܝܡܢܘܬܐ ܗܝܕܝܢ ܩܡ ܘܟܐܐ ܒܪܘܚܐ ܘܒܝܡܐ ܘܗܘܐ ܫܠܝܐ ܪܒܐ
verses.el: καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς Τί δειλοί ἐστε, ὀλιγόπιστοι; τότε ἐγερθεὶς ἐπετίμησεν τοῖς ἀνέμοις καὶ τῇ θαλάσσῃ, καὶ ἐγένετο γαλήνη μεγάλη.
verses.en: Yeshua said to them: Why are you fearful, O small of trust? Then he arose and rebuked the wind and the sea, and there was a great stillness.
verses.ar: قالَ لَهُم يَشوع: لِماذا تَجبُنون يا قَليلي الإيمان؟ ثُمَّ قامَ وانتَهَرَ الرِّياحَ والبَحر، فصارَ هُدوءٌ عَظيم.

verses.syr: ܐܢܫܐ ܕܝܢ ܐܬܕܡܪܘ ܘܐܡܪܝܢ ܡܢܘ ܗܢܐ ܕܪܘܚܐ ܘܝܡܐ ܡܫܬܡܥܝܢ ܠܗ
verses.el: οἱ δὲ ἄνθρωποι ἐθαύμασαν λέγοντες Ποταπός ἐστιν οὗτος ὅτι καὶ οἱ ἄνεμοι καὶ ἡ θάλασσα αὐτῷ ὑπακούουσιν;
verses.en: And the men marveled and said: Who is this, that the wind and the sea obey him?
verses.ar: فتَعَجَّبَ الرِّجالُ وقالوا: مَن هذا الذي تُطيعُهُ الرِّياحُ والبَحر؟

verses.syr: ܘܟܕ ܐܬܐ ܝܫܘܥ ܠܥܒܪܐ ܠܐܬܪܐ ܕܓܕܪܝܐ ܐܪܥܘܗܝ ܬܪܝܢ ܕܝܘܢܐ ܕܢܦܩܝܢ ܡܢ ܒܝܬ ܩܒܘܪܐ ܒܝܫܐ ܕܛܒ ܐܝܟ ܕܠܐ ܐܢܫ ܢܫܟܚ ܢܥܒܪ ܒܗܝ ܐܘܪܚܐ
verses.el: Καὶ ἐλθόντος αὐτοῦ εἰς τὸ πέραν εἰς τὴν χώραν τῶν Γαδαρηνῶν ὑπήντησαν αὐτῷ δύο δαιμονιζόμενοι ἐκ τῶν μνημείων ἐξερχόμενοι, χαλεποὶ λίαν ὥστε μὴ ἰσχύειν τινὰ παρελθεῖν διὰ τῆς ὁδοῦ ἐκείνης.
verses.en: And when Yeshua came to the other side, to the region of the Gadarenes, two who had demons met him, coming out from the tombs, exceedingly fierce, so that no one could pass by that road.
verses.ar: ولمّا جاءَ يَشوعُ إلى الجانِبِ الآخَر، إلى كُورةِ الجَدَريِّين، استَقبَلَهُ مَجنونانِ خارِجانِ مِن المَقابِر، شَديدانِ جِدًّا، حتّى لم يَستَطِع أحَدٌ أن يَجتازَ مِن تِلكَ الطَّريق.
