verses.ar: مِنهُنَّ مَريَمُ المَجدَلِيَّةُ، ومَريَمُ أُمُّ يَعقوبَ ويوسُفَ، وأُمُّ ابنَي زَبَدي.

verses.syr: ܟܕ ܗܘܐ ܕܝܢ ܪܡܫܐ ܐܬܐ ܓܒܪܐ ܥܬܝܪܐ ܡܢ ܪܡܬܐ ܕܫܡܗ ܝܘܣܦ ܕܐܦ ܗܘ ܐܬܬܠܡܕ ܗܘܐ ܠܝܫܘܥ
verses.el: Ὀψίας δὲ γενομένης ἦλθεν ἄνθρωπος πλούσιος ἀπὸ Ἀριμαθαίας, τοὔνομα Ἰωσήφ, ὃς καὶ αὐτὸς ἐμαθητεύθη τῷ Ἰησοῦ·
verses.en: And when evening came, a wealthy man from Ramatah named Yosef came — who had himself also become a disciple of Yeshua.
verses.ar: ولمّا أمسى جاءَ رَجُلٌ غَنِيٌّ مِنَ الرّامَةِ يُقالُ لَهُ يوسُفُ — وكانَ قَد صارَ هوَ أيضًا تِلميذًا لِيَشوعَ.

verses.syr: ܗܢܐ ܩܪܒ ܠܘܬ ܦܝܠܛܘܣ ܘܫܐܠ ܦܓܪܗ ܕܝܫܘܥ ܘܦܩܕ ܦܝܠܛܘܣ ܕܢܬܝܗܒ ܠܗ ܦܓܪܐ
verses.el: οὗτος προσελθὼν τῷ Πειλάτῳ ᾐτήσατο τὸ σῶμα τοῦ Ἰησοῦ. τότε ὁ Πειλᾶτος ἐκέλευσεν ἀποδοθῆναι.
verses.en: He drew near to Pilatus and asked for the body of Yeshua, and Pilatus commanded that the body be given to him.
verses.ar: فدَنا مِن بيلاطُسَ وطَلَبَ جَسَدَ يَشوعَ، فأمَرَ بيلاطُسُ بِتَسليمِهِ.

verses.syr: ܘܫܩܠܗ ܝܘܣܦ ܠܦܓܪܐ ܘܟܪܟܗ ܒܚܝܨܐ ܕܟܬܢܐ ܢܩܕܐ
verses.el: καὶ λαβὼν τὸ σῶμα ὁ Ἰωσὴφ ἐνετύλιξεν αὐτὸ ἐν σινδόνι καθαρᾷ,
verses.en: And Yosef took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth
verses.ar: فأخَذَ يوسُفُ الجَسَدَ ولَفَّهُ بِكَتّانٍ نَقِيٍّ

verses.syr: ܘܣܡܗ ܒܒܝܬ ܩܒܘܪܐ ܚܕܬܐ ܕܝܠܗ ܕܢܩܝܪ ܒܟܐܦܐ ܘܥܓܠܘ ܟܐܦܐ ܪܒܬܐ ܐܪܡܝܘ ܥܠ ܬܪܥܐ ܕܒܝܬ ܩܒܘܪܐ ܘܐܙܠܘ
verses.el: καὶ ἔθηκεν αὐτὸ ἐν τῷ καινῷ αὐτοῦ μνημείῳ ὃ ἐλατόμησεν ἐν τῇ πέτρᾳ, καὶ προσκυλίσας λίθον μέγαν τῇ θύρᾳ τοῦ μνημείου ἀπῆλθεν.
verses.en: and placed it in his own new tomb, which had been hewn in the rock. And they rolled a great stone against the door of the tomb and departed.
verses.ar: ووَضَعَهُ في قَبرِهِ الجَديدِ الَّذي كانَ قَد نَحَتَهُ في الصَّخرَةِ. ودَحرَجَ حَجَرًا كَبيرًا على بابِ القَبرِ وانصَرَفَ.

verses.syr: ܐܝܬ ܗܘܝ ܕܝܢ ܬܡܢ ܡܪܝܡ ܡܓܕܠܝܬܐ ܘܡܪܝܡ ܐܚܪܬܐ ܕܝܬܒܢ ܗܘܝ ܠܩܘܒܠܗ ܕܩܒܪܐ
verses.el: Ἦν δὲ ἐκεῖ Μαριὰμ ἡ Μαγδαληνὴ καὶ ἡ ἄλλη Μαρία, καθήμεναι ἀπέναντι τοῦ τάφου.
verses.en: And there was Maryam of Magdala, and the other Maryam, sitting there before the tomb.
verses.ar: وكانَت هُناكَ مَريَمُ المَجدَلِيَّةُ ومَريَمُ الأُخرى جالِسَتَينِ أمامَ القَبرِ.

verses.syr: ܠܝܘܡܐ ܕܝܢ ܕܡܚܪ ܕܐܝܬܘܗܝ ܒܬܪ ܥܪܘܒܬܐ ܐܬܟܢܫܘ ܪܒܝ ܟܗܢܐ ܘܦܪܝܫܐ ܠܘܬ ܦܝܠܛܘܣ
verses.el: Τῇ δὲ ἐπαύριον, ἥτις ἐστὶν μετὰ τὴν Παρασκευήν, συνήχθησαν οἱ ἀρχιερεῖς καὶ οἱ Φαρισαῖοι πρὸς Πειλᾶτον
verses.en: On the next day — which is after the Preparation — the chief priests and the Separatists gathered together to Pilatus,
verses.ar: وفي اليَومِ التّالي — الَّذي هوَ بَعدَ الاستِعدادِ — اجتَمَعَ رُؤساءُ الكَهَنَةِ والفَرِّيسيّونَ إلى بيلاطُسَ

verses.syr: ܘܐܡܪܝܢ ܠܗ ܡܪܢ ܐܬܕܟܪܢ ܕܗܘ ܡܛܥܝܢܐ ܐܡܪ ܗܘܐ ܟܕ ܚܝ ܕܡܢ ܒܬܪ ܬܠܬܐ ܝܘܡܝܢ ܩܐܡ ܐܢܐ
verses.el: λέγοντες Κύριε, ἐμνήσθημεν ὅτι ἐκεῖνος ὁ πλάνος εἶπεν ἔτι ζῶν Μετὰ τρεῖς ἡμέρας ἐγείρομαι.
verses.en: saying to him: Our lord, we remember that that deceiver said while he was living: After three days I will rise.
verses.ar: قائِلينَ: يا سيِّدَنا، تَذَكَّرنا أنَّ ذلِكَ المُضَلِّلَ قالَ وهوَ حَيٌّ: بَعدَ ثَلاثَةِ أيّامٍ أقومُ.

verses.syr: ܦܩܘܕ ܗܟܝܠ ܡܙܕܗܪܝܢ ܒܩܒܪܐ ܥܕܡܐ ܠܬܠܬܐ ܝܘܡܝܢ ܕܠܡܐ ܢܐܬܘܢ ܬܠܡܝܕܘܗܝ ܢܓܢܒܘܢܝܗܝ ܒܠܠܝܐ ܘܢܐܡܪܘܢ ܠܥܡܐ ܕܡܢ ܒܝܬ ܡܝܬܐ ܩܡ ܘܬܗܘܐ ܛܘܥܝܝ ܐܚܪܝܬܐ ܒܝܫܐ ܡܢ ܩܕܡܝܬܐ
verses.el: κέλευσον οὖν ἀσφαλισθῆναι τὸν τάφον ἕως τῆς τρίτης ἡμέρας, μή ποτε ἐλθόντες οἱ μαθηταὶ κλέψωσιν αὐτὸν καὶ εἴπωσιν τῷ λαῷ Ἠγέρθη ἀπὸ τῶν νεκρῶν, καὶ ἔσται ἡ ἐσχάτη πλάνη χείρων τῆς πρώτης.
verses.en: Command therefore that the tomb be made secure until the third day, lest his disciples come and steal him by night and say to the people: He has risen from the dead — and the last deception would be worse than the first.
verses.ar: فأمُر إذَن بِتَأمينِ القَبرِ حتّى اليَومِ الثّالِثِ لِئَلّا يَأتيَ تَلامِيذُهُ لَيلًا ويَسرِقوهُ ثُمَّ يَقولوا لِلشَّعبِ: قامَ مِنَ الأمواتِ — فتَكونَ الضَّلالَةُ الأخيرَةُ أشَدَّ مِنَ الأولى.

verses.syr: ܐܡܪ ܠܗܘܢ ܦܝܠܛܘܣ ܐܝܬ ܠܟܘܢ ܩܣܛܘܢܪܐ ܙܠܘ ܐܙܕܗܪܘ ܐܝܟܢܐ ܕܝܕܥܝܢ ܐܢܬܘܢ
verses.el: ἔφη αὐτοῖς ὁ Πειλᾶτος Ἔχετε κουστωδίαν· ὑπάγετε ἀσφαλίσασθε ὡς οἴδατε.
verses.en: Pilatus said to them: You have a guard — go, make it secure as you know how.
verses.ar: قالَ لَهُم بيلاطُسُ: لَدَيكُم حَرَسٌ — اذهَبوا أمِّنوهُ كَما تَعلَمونَ.

verses.syr: ܗܢܘܢ ܕܝܢ ܐܙܠܘ ܐܙܕܗܪܘ ܒܩܒܪܐ ܘܚܬܡܘ ܟܐܦܐ ܗܝ ܥܡ ܩܣܛܘܢܪܐ
verses.el: οἱ δὲ πορευθέντες ἠσφαλίσαντο τὸν τάφον σφραγίσαντες τὸν λίθον μετὰ τῆς κουστωδίας.
verses.en: And they went and made the tomb secure, sealing the stone together with the guard.
verses.ar: فذَهَبوا وأمَّنوا القَبرَ خاتِمينَ الحَجَرَ مَعَ الحَرَسِ.

chapters.commentary:
# Annotations on Matthew Chapter 27 — The Trial, the Impaling, and the Burial

## Syriac-First Approach

This chapter is translated primarily from the Syriac Peshitta. The passion narrative reaches its culmination with the cry from the cross — ܐܠܗܝ ܐܠܗܝ ܠܡܢܐ ܫܒܩܬܢܝ (Eli, Eli, lema shevaqtani) — whose central word ܫܒܩ (shvaq, release) is the same root woven through the Lord's Prayer and the cup-saying of chapter 26. The cross-cry is the theological center of Matthew's passion.

## Root-Recovery Choices

### v.4: ܚܛܝܬ (ḥaṭit) — "I have missed the mark"
Root ܚ-ܛ-ܐ (ḥ-ṭ-'): to miss, to err, to go astray. ܚܛܝܬ (ḥaṭit) = "I have missed the mark" — Yehudah's confession to the priests. This is the same root as ܚܛܗܐ (ḥaṭahe, sins/misses) in the cup-saying (26:28: "for the release of missing-the-mark"). Yehudah's act is his own ḥaṭa — and the blood he handed over was for the release of exactly this. The irony is structural.

### v.5: ܚܢܩ ܢܦܫܗ (ḥanaq nafsheh) — "strangled his breath-life"
Root ܚ-ܢ-ܩ (ḥ-n-q): to strangle, to choke. ܢܦܫܐ (nafsha): breath/life. To strangle one's breath — the act of suicide is rendered literally: he extinguished his own breath. Compare: "whoever loses his ܢܦܫ on my account shall find it" (16:25) — Yehudah loses his ܢܦܫ in a very different sense.

### v.16: ܒܪ ܐܒܐ (Bar-Abba) — "son of the father"
ܒܪ (bar): son. ܐܒܐ (aba): father. Bar-Abba = "son of the father." The crowd releases Bar-Abba (son of the father) and condemns Yeshua, who is also called "Son of the Father" (God). The release of one son-of-the-father and the condemnation of the other is one of Matthew's richest ironies. Some manuscripts give Bar-Abba's first name as Yeshua — "Yeshua Bar-Abba" — making the juxtaposition even more precise.

### v.22: ܢܨܛܠܒ (netstalev) — "let him be impaled"
Root ܨ-ܠ-ܒ (ṣ-l-b): to impale, to crucify. ܨܠܝܒܐ (ṣaliba) = the impaling-beam, the cross. The root-recovery translation uses "impale" throughout because crucifixion was the Roman method of impaling on a stake/cross — a technical term that has been conventionalized into "crucify," which has lost its physical meaning. The Syriac ܨܠܝܒ preserves the physicality.

### v.24: ܙܕܝܩ (zaddiq) — "righteous/right"
Root ܙ-ܕ-ܩ (z-d-q): rightness, justice. Pilatus calls Yeshua ܙܕܝܩ (a right man) while washing his hands. The Syriac preserves the key root-recovery term: not merely "innocent" (Greek ἀθῷος, athoios = unpunishable) but ܙܕܝܩ — aligned with rightness. The pagan governor uses Israel's core theological term for the one Israel condemns.

### v.29: ܕܝܩܐ ܕܟܘܒܐ (diqe d-kube) — "crown of thorns"
ܕܝܩܐ (diqa): crown, diadem. Root ܕ-ܝ-ܩ (d-y-q): to adorn the head. ܟܘܒܐ (kuba): thorn, bramble. The crown of thorns is a mock royal installation — the soldiers perform an anti-coronation. The materials of the curse (Genesis 3:18: "thorns and thistles") become the crown of the king.

### v.33: ܓܓܘܠܬܐ (Gagulta) — "the Skull"
ܓܓܘܠܬܐ (Gagulta): skull. Hebrew גלגלת (gulgoleth). Greek Γολγοθᾶ (Golgotha), then "Calvaria" in Latin (hence Calvary). The "Place of the Skull" — the rendering of this physical place-name is consistent: it is a skull-shaped hill, or a place of skulls.

### v.46: ܐܠܗܝ ܐܠܗܝ ܠܡܢܐ ܫܒܩܬܢܝ (Eli, Eli, lema shevaqtani) — **THE CENTRAL CRY**
**THIS IS THE THEOLOGICAL CENTER OF MATTHEW'S PASSION.**

ܫܒܩ (shvaq): release, let go, forgive, abandon.

The same ܫܒܩ that:
- "Release us our ܚܘܒܐ (debts)" — Lord's Prayer 6:12
- "Whatever you ܫܒܩ on earth shall be released in heaven" — 16:19, 18:18
- "How many times shall I ܫܒܩ my brother?" — 18:21
- "My blood... for the ܫܒܩ of ܚܛܗܐ (missing-the-mark)" — 26:28
- "Eli, Eli, lema ܫܒܩܬܢܝ?" — 27:46

This is the Lord's Prayer from the cross. "Why have You RELEASED me?" — using the very word Yeshua taught his disciples to use of God releasing their debts. Now, in the moment of maximal abandonment, he cries the release-word back to God as a question: Why have you released/abandoned me?

The conventional "forsaken" is technically accurate but misses the echo. "Released" makes the structural connection visible: the one who prayed "release us our debts" is himself released — let go — into death. The release he gave (18:21-35) and the release his blood purchases (26:28) is the release he experiences.

The cry quotes Psalm 22:1 (מי א-ל מי א-ל למה עזבתני, Eli Eli lama azavtani). The Syriac ܫܒܩܬܢܝ (shevaqtani) translates עזבתני (azavtani, abandoned/left me) — but ܫܒܩ enriches it: the Syriac word carries both abandonment AND release, both being let go AND being forgiven. He is released — in all senses.

### v.50: ܫܪܐ ܪܘܚܗ (shre ruḥeh) — "released his breath"
Root ܫ-ܪ-ܐ (sh-r-'): to release, to loose. ܪܘܚ (ruḥ): breath, spirit. "Yeshua again cried out with a loud voice and ܫܪܐ ܪܘܚܗ (released his breath)." Not "gave up the ghost" (archaic English) or even "breathed his last" — he RELEASED his breath. The same release-vocabulary continues: he releases his breath at the moment he has been released. Greek ἀφῆκεν τὸ πνεῦμα (aphēken to pneuma) = "sent away/released his spirit." The Syriac and Greek converge: the breath is released.

### v.51: ܦܪܣܦܐ ܕܗܝܟܠܐ (parsphe d-haykla) — "veil of the Temple"
ܦܪܣܦܐ (parspa): veil, curtain. Root related to Persian parda (curtain/veil). The veil between the outer courts and the Holy of Holies — the threshold that only the High Priest crossed, once a year. It tears "from top to bottom" — not from human hands but from above. The separation between the sacred interior and the world is torn open from the inside.

## Cross-Scriptural Root Connections

| Syriac | Hebrew | Arabic | Shared meaning | Qur'an connection |
|--------|--------|--------|----------------|-------------------|
| ܫܒܩ (shvaq) | עזב (azav)/שבק | ترك (taraka) | release/abandon | — |
| ܙܕܩ (zaddiq) | צדיק (tsaddiq) | صادق (ṣādiq) | righteous/right | 4:135 |
| ܢܦܫ (nafsha) | נפש (nefesh) | نفس (nafs) | breath/life | 89:27 |
| ܪܘܚ (ruḥa) | רוח (ruaḥ) | روح (rūḥ) | breath/spirit | 17:85 |
| ܚܛ (ḥaṭ) | חטא (ḥaṭa) | خطأ (khaṭaʾ) | miss the mark/error | 4:112 |

## Bahá'í Interpretive Notes

The passion of Yeshua — the trial, the impaling, the death — is understood in Bahá'í interpretation as the archetype of what Bahá'u'lláh calls "the most great oppression": the Manifestation of God handed over by the religious authorities to the civil power and executed. Bahá'u'lláh writes in the Íqán that every Manifestation has drunk from the "cup of affliction" poured by those who should have recognized him. The cry from the cross — "why have You released me?" — is the expression of the Manifestation's human station experiencing the full weight of abandonment, even as the divine station remains intact. 'Abdu'l-Bahá notes that the tearing of the Temple veil symbolizes the end of exclusive access to the divine — henceforth the Holy of Holies is open to all. The centurion's confession ("truly this was a son of God," v.54) represents the Gentile/outsider recognition that precedes the community's.

## Integrative Links

- **v.9-10 thirty pieces of silver ↔ Zechariah 11:12-13**: "They weighed out my price — thirty pieces of silver... and I took the thirty pieces of silver and threw them to the potter." Matthew quotes this passage as fulfilled in Yehudah's act.
- **v.34 vinegar/gall ↔ Psalm 69:21**: "For my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink." The Psalm of the suffering righteous one is being enacted verse by verse.
- **v.35 dividing garments ↔ Psalm 22:18**: "They divide my garments among them and for my clothing cast lots." Matthew's passion narrative is a living Psalm 22.
- **v.46 cry from the cross ↔ Psalm 22:1**: ʾĒlī ʾĒlī lāmā ʿăzaḇtānī — the Hebrew original. Yeshua's Aramaic/Syriac version uses ܫܒܩ for עזב, enriching the abandoned/released resonance.
- **v.46 shevaqtani ↔ Lord's Prayer 6:12**: ܫܒܩ ܠܢ ܚܘܒܝܢ — the release-word completes its circle: from the prayer to the cross.
- **v.51 veil torn ↔ Qur'an 33:53**: حجاب (ḥijāb) — the veil between sacred and profane. When the veil is torn, direct access to the divine is no longer mediated by priestly gatekeepers.

chapters.title: Matthew — ܩܦܠܐܘܢ 28
chapters.subtitle: Matthew — Chapter 28

verses.syr: ܒܪܡܫܐ ܕܝܢ ܒܫܒܬܐ ܕܢܓܗ ܚܕ ܒܫܒܐ ܐܬܬ ܡܪܝܡ ܡܓܕܠܝܬܐ ܘܡܪܝܡ ܐܚܪܬܐ ܕܢܚܙܝܢ ܩܒܪܐ
verses.el: Ὀψὲ δὲ σαββάτων, τῇ ἐπιφωσκούσῃ εἰς μίαν σαββάτων, ἦλθεν Μαριὰμ ἡ Μαγδαληνὴ καὶ ἡ ἄλλη Μαρία θεωρῆσαι τὸν τάφον.
verses.en: And at the evening of the Sabbath, as it dawned toward the first day of the week, Maryam of Magdala and the other Maryam came to see the tomb.
verses.ar: وفي عَشِيَّةِ السَّبتِ حينَ أضاءَ أوَّلُ أيّامِ الأُسبوعِ جاءَت مَريَمُ المَجدَلِيَّةُ ومَريَمُ الأُخرى لِتَنظُرا القَبرَ.

verses.syr: ܘܗܐ ܙܘܥܐ ܪܒܐ ܗܘܐ ܡܠܐܟܐ ܓܝܪ ܕܡܪܝܐ ܢܚܬ ܡܢ ܫܡܝܐ ܘܩܪܒ ܥܓܠ ܟܐܦܐ ܡܢ ܬܪܥܐ ܘܝܬܒ ܗܘܐ ܥܠܝܗ
verses.el: καὶ ἰδοὺ σεισμὸς ἐγένετο μέγας· ἄγγελος γὰρ Κυρίου καταβὰς ἐξ οὐρανοῦ καὶ προσελθὼν ἀπεκύλισεν τὸν λίθον καὶ ἐκάθητο ἐπάνω αὐτοῦ.
verses.en: And behold, a great earthquake had been — for an angel of the Lord had descended from the heavens and drawn near and rolled the stone from the door and was sitting upon it.
verses.ar: وإذا بِزَلزَلَةٍ عَظيمَةٍ قَد كانَت — فإنَّ مَلاكًا مِنَ الرَّبِّ نَزَلَ مِنَ السَّماواتِ ودَنا ودَحرَجَ الحَجَرَ عَن البابِ وكانَ جالِسًا عَلَيهِ.

verses.syr: ܐܝܬܘܗܝ ܗܘܐ ܕܝܢ ܚܙܘܗ ܐܝܟ ܒܪܩܐ ܘܠܒܘܫܗ ܚܘܪ ܗܘܐ ܐܝܟ ܬܠܓܐ
verses.el: ἦν δὲ ἡ εἰδέα αὐτοῦ ὡς ἀστραπὴ, καὶ τὸ ἔνδυμα αὐτοῦ λευκὸν ὡς χιών.
verses.en: And his appearance was like lightning, and his garment was white as snow.
verses.ar: وكانَ مَنظَرُهُ كالبَرقِ وثَوبُهُ أبيَضُ كالثَّلجِ.

verses.syr: ܘܡܢ ܕܚܠܬܗ ܐܬܬܙܝܥܘ ܐܝܠܝܢ ܕܢܛܪܝܢ ܗܘܘ ܘܗܘܘ ܐܝܟ ܡܝܬܐ
verses.el: ἀπὸ δὲ τοῦ φόβου αὐτοῦ ἐσείσθησαν οἱ τηροῦντες καὶ ἐγενήθησαν ὡς νεκροί.
verses.en: And from fear of him those who were guarding trembled and became as the dead.
verses.ar: ومِن خَوفِهِ ارتَجَفَ الحُرّاسُ وصاروا كالأمواتِ.

verses.syr: ܥܢܐ ܕܝܢ ܡܠܐܟܐ ܘܐܡܪ ܠܢܫܐ ܐܢܬܝܢ ܠܐ ܬܕܚܠܢ ܝܕܥ ܐܢܐ ܓܝܪ ܕܠܝܫܘܥ ܕܐܙܕܩܦ ܒܥܝܢ ܐܢܬܝܢ
verses.el: ἀποκριθεὶς δὲ ὁ ἄγγελος εἶπεν ταῖς γυναιξίν Μὴ φοβεῖσθε ὑμεῖς· οἶδα γὰρ ὅτι Ἰησοῦν τὸν ἐσταυρωμένον ζητεῖτε·
verses.en: But the angel answered and said to the women: You — do not fear! For I know that you seek Yeshua who was impaled.
verses.ar: فأجابَ المَلاكُ وقالَ لِلنِّساءِ: أنتُنَّ — لا تَخَفنَ! فإنِّي أعلَمُ أنَّكُنَّ تَطلُبنَ يَشوعَ المَصلوبَ.

verses.syr: ܠܐ ܗܘܐ ܬܢܢ ܩܡ ܠܗ ܓܝܪ ܐܝܟܢܐ ܕܐܡܪ ܬܐܝܝܢ ܚܙܝܝܢ ܕܘܟܬܐ ܕܣܝܡ ܗܘܐ ܒܗ ܡܪܢ
verses.el: οὐκ ἔστιν ὧδε· ἠγέρθη γὰρ καθὼς εἶπεν· δεῦτε ἴδετε τὸν τόπον ὅπου ἔκειτο.
verses.en: He is not here — for he has risen, just as he said. Come, see the place where our Lord lay.
verses.ar: لَيسَ هوَ هنا — فإنَّهُ قامَ كَما قالَ. هَلُمَّ انظُرا المَكانَ الَّذي كانَ فيهِ سيِّدُنا.

verses.syr: ܘܙܠܝܢ ܒܥܓܠ ܐܡܪܝܢ ܠܬܠܡܝܕܘܗܝ ܕܩܡ ܡܢ ܒܝܬ ܡܝܬܐ ܘܗܐ ܩܕܡ ܠܟܘܢ ܠܓܠܝܠܐ ܬܡܢ ܬܚܙܘܢܝܗܝ ܗܐ ܐܡܪܬ ܠܟܝܢ
verses.el: καὶ ταχὺ πορευθεῖσαι εἴπατε τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ ὅτι Ἠγέρθη ἀπὸ τῶν νεκρῶν, καὶ ἰδοὺ προάγει ὑμᾶς εἰς τὴν Γαλιλαίαν, ἐκεῖ αὐτὸν ὄψεσθε. ἰδοὺ εἶπον ὑμῖν.
verses.en: And go quickly and tell his disciples that he has risen from among the dead, and behold, he goes before you to the Galil — there you shall see him. Behold, I have told you.
verses.ar: واذهَبا بِسُرعَةٍ وأخبِرا تَلامِيذَهُ أنَّهُ قامَ مِنَ الأمواتِ، وها يَسبِقُكُنَّ إلى الجَليلِ — هُناكَ تَرَيانِهِ. ها قَد أخبَرتُكُنَّ.

verses.syr: ܘܐܙܠ ܥܓܠ ܡܢ ܩܒܪܐ ܒܕܚܠܬܐ ܘܒܚܕܘܬܐ ܪܒܬܐ ܘܪܗܛܢ ܕܢܐܡܪܢ ܠܬܠܡܝܕܘܗܝ
verses.el: καὶ ἀπελθοῦσαι ταχὺ ἀπὸ τοῦ μνημείου μετὰ φόβου καὶ χαρᾶς μεγάλης ἔδραμον ἀπαγγεῖλαι τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ.
verses.en: And they went quickly from the tomb with fear and great joy, and ran to tell his disciples.
verses.ar: فخَرَجتا بِسُرعَةٍ مِنَ القَبرِ بِخَوفٍ وفَرَحٍ عَظيمٍ، وركَضَتا لِتُخبِرا تَلامِيذَهُ.

verses.syr: ܘܗܐ ܝܫܘܥ ܦܓܥ ܒܗܝܢ ܘܐܡܪ ܠܗܝܢ ܫܠܡ ܠܟܝܢ ܗܢܝܢ ܕܝܢ ܩܪܒ ܐܚܕ ܪܓܠܘܗܝ ܘܣܓܕܝܢ ܠܗ
verses.el: καὶ ἰδοὺ Ἰησοῦς ὑπήντησεν αὐταῖς λέγων Χαίρετε. αἱ δὲ προσελθοῦσαι ἐκράτησαν αὐτοῦ τοὺς πόδας καὶ προσεκύνησαν αὐτῷ.
verses.en: And behold, Yeshua met them and said to them: Peace to you! And they drew near, took hold of his feet, and bowed down to him.
verses.ar: وإذا بِيَشوعَ قَد لَقِيَهُنَّ وقالَ لَهُنَّ: السَّلامُ عَلَيكُنَّ! فدَنَوتا وأمسَكَتا بِقَدَمَيهِ وسَجَدَتا لَهُ.

verses.syr: ܗܝܕܝܢ ܐܡܪ ܠܗܝܢ ܝܫܘܥ ܠܐ ܬܕܚܠܢ ܐܠܐ ܙܠܝܢ ܐܡܪܝܢ ܠܐܚܝ ܕܢܐܙܠܘܢ ܠܓܠܝܠܐ ܘܬܡܢ ܢܚܙܘܢܢܝ
verses.el: τότε λέγει αὐταῖς ὁ Ἰησοῦς Μὴ φοβεῖσθε· ὑπάγετε ἀπαγγείλατε τοῖς ἀδελφοῖς μου ἵνα ἀπέλθωσιν εἰς τὴν Γαλιλαίαν, κἀκεῖ με ὄψονται.
verses.en: Then Yeshua said to them: Do not fear — but go, tell my brothers to go to the Galil, and there they shall see me.
verses.ar: حينَئِذٍ قالَ لَهُنَّ يَشوعُ: لا تَخَفنَ — بَل اذهَبا أخبِرا إخوَتي أن يَذهَبوا إلى الجَليلِ وهُناكَ يَرَونَني.

verses.syr: ܟܕ ܐܙܠܝܢ ܕܝܢ ܐܬܘ ܐܢܫܐ ܡܢ ܩܣܛܘܢܪܐ ܗܢܘܢ ܠܡܕܝܢܬܐ ܘܐܡܪܘ ܠܪܒܝ ܟܗܢܐ ܟܠ ܡܕܡ ܕܗܘܐ
verses.el: Πορευομένων δὲ αὐτῶν ἰδού τινες τῆς κουστωδίας ἐλθόντες εἰς τὴν πόλιν ἀπήγγειλαν τοῖς ἀρχιερεῦσιν ἅπαντα τὰ γενόμενα.
verses.en: And while they were going, some of those of the guard came into the city and reported to the chief priests all that had happened.
verses.ar: وبَينَما هُنَّ ذاهِباتٌ إذا بِبَعضِ الحَرَسِ قَد دَخَلوا المَدينَةَ وأخبَروا رُؤساءَ الكَهَنَةِ بِكُلِّ ما جَرى.

verses.syr: ܘܐܬܟܢܫܘ ܥܡ ܩܫܝܫܐ ܘܢܣܒܘ ܡܠܟܐ ܘܝܗܒܘ ܟܣܦܐ ܠܐ ܙܥܘܪ ܠܩܣܛܘܢܪܐ
verses.el: καὶ συναχθέντες μετὰ τῶν πρεσβυτέρων συμβούλιόν τε λαβόντες ἀργύρια ἱκανὰ ἔδωκαν τοῖς στρατιώταις
verses.en: And they gathered with the elders and took counsel and gave a not-small amount of silver to the soldiers,
verses.ar: فاجتَمَعوا مَعَ الشُّيوخِ وتَشاوَروا وأعطَوا الجُندَ فِضَّةً غيرَ قَليلَةٍ،

verses.syr: ܘܐܡܪܝܢ ܠܗܘܢ ܐܡܪܘ ܕܬܠܡܝܕܘܗܝ ܐܬܘ ܓܢܒܘܗܝ ܒܠܠܝܐ ܟܕ ܕܡܟܝܢ ܚܢܢ
verses.el: λέγοντες Εἴπατε ὅτι Οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ νυκτὸς ἐλθόντες ἔκλεψαν αὐτὸν ἡμῶν κοιμωμένων.
verses.en: saying to them: Say — his disciples came by night and stole him while we were sleeping.
verses.ar: قائِلينَ: قولوا — إنَّ تَلامِيذَهُ جاؤوا لَيلًا وسَرَقوهُ ونَحنُ نائِمونَ.

verses.syr: ܘܐܢ ܐܫܬܡܥܬ ܗܕܐ ܩܕܡ ܗܓܡܘܢܐ ܚܢܢ ܡܦܝܣܝܢ ܚܢܢ ܠܗ ܘܠܟܘܢ ܕܠܐ ܨܦܬܐ ܥܒܕܝܢ ܚܢܢ
verses.el: καὶ ἐὰν ἀκουσθῇ τοῦτο ἐπὶ τοῦ ἡγεμόνος, ἡμεῖς πείσομεν καὶ ὑμᾶς ἀμερίμνους ποιήσομεν.
verses.en: And if this is heard before the governor, we shall persuade him, and you — we shall make you free of care.
verses.ar: وإن بَلَغَ هذا إلى مَسامِعِ الوالي نُقنِعُهُ وأنتُم — نُريحُكُم مِنَ القَلَقِ.

verses.syr: ܗܢܘܢ ܕܝܢ ܟܕ ܢܣܒܘ ܟܣܦܐ ܥܒܕܘ ܐܝܟ ܕܐܠܦܘ ܐܢܘܢ ܘܢܦܩܬ ܡܠܬܐ ܗܕܐ ܒܝܬ ܝܗܘܕܝܐ ܥܕܡܐ ܠܝܘܡܢܐ
verses.el: οἱ δὲ λαβόντες ἀργύρια ἐποίησαν ὡς ἐδιδάχθησαν. Καὶ διεφημίσθη ὁ λόγος οὗτος παρὰ Ἰουδαίοις μέχρι τῆς σήμερον ἡμέρας.
verses.en: They took the silver and did as they were taught, and this word spread among the Yehudim until today.
verses.ar: فأخَذوا الفِضَّةَ وفَعَلوا كَما عُلِّموا. وذاعَ هذا الكَلامُ بَينَ اليَهودِ إلى اليَومِ.

verses.syr: ܬܠܡܝܕܐ ܕܝܢ ܚܕܥܣܪ ܐܙܠܘ ܠܓܠܝܠܐ ܠܛܘܪܐ ܐܝܟܐ ܕܘܥܕ ܐܢܘܢ ܝܫܘܥ
verses.el: Οἱ δὲ ἕνδεκα μαθηταὶ ἐπορεύθησαν εἰς τὴν Γαλιλαίαν, εἰς τὸ ὄρος οὗ ἐτάξατο αὐτοῖς ὁ Ἰησοῦς,
verses.en: And the eleven disciples went to the Galil, to the mountain where Yeshua had appointed them.
verses.ar: وذَهَبَ التَّلامِيذُ الأحَدَ عَشَرَ إلى الجَليلِ إلى الجَبَلِ الَّذي عَيَّنَهُ لَهُم يَشوعُ.

verses.syr: ܘܟܕ ܚܙܐܘܗܝ ܣܓܕܘ ܠܗ ܡܢܗܘܢ ܕܝܢ ܐܬܦܠܓܘ ܗܘܘ
verses.el: καὶ ἰδόντες αὐτὸν προσεκύνησαν, οἱ δὲ ἐδίστασαν.
verses.en: And when they saw him they bowed down to him, though some among them wavered.
verses.ar: فلمّا رَأوهُ سَجَدوا لَهُ، وإن كانَ بَعضٌ مِنهُم تَرَدَّدَ.

verses.syr: ܘܩܪܒ ܝܫܘܥ ܡܠܠ ܥܡܗܘܢ ܘܐܡܪ ܠܗܘܢ ܐܬܝܗܒ ܠܝ ܟܠ ܫܘܠܛܢ ܒܫܡܝܐ ܘܒܐܪܥܐ ܘܐܝܟܢܐ ܕܫܕܪܢܝ ܐܒܝ ܡܫܕܪ ܐܢܐ ܠܟܘܢ
verses.el: καὶ προσελθὼν ὁ Ἰησοῦς ἐλάλησεν αὐτοῖς λέγων Ἐδόθη μοι πᾶσα ἐξουσία ἐν οὐρανῷ καὶ ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς.
verses.en: And Yeshua drew near and spoke with them and said to them: All authority has been given to me in the heavens and on the earth — and as my Father has sent me, I send you.
verses.ar: فدَنا يَشوعُ وتَكَلَّمَ مَعَهُم وقالَ لَهُم: أُعطِيَت لي كُلُّ سُلطانٍ في السَّماواتِ وعلى الأرضِ — وكَما أرسَلَني أبي أُرسِلُكُم.

verses.syr: ܙܠܘ ܗܟܝܠ ܬܠܡܕܘ ܟܠܗܘܢ ܥܡܡܐ ܘܐܥܡܕܘ ܐܢܘܢ ܒܫܡ ܐܒܐ ܘܒܪܐ ܘܪܘܚܐ ܕܩܘܕܫܐ
verses.el: πορευθέντες οὖν μαθητεύσατε πάντα τὰ ἔθνη, βαπτίζοντες αὐτοὺς εἰς τὸ ὄνομα τοῦ Πατρὸς καὶ τοῦ Υἱοῦ καὶ τοῦ Ἁγίου Πνεύματος,
verses.en: Go therefore and make disciples of all the nations, and immerse them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Breath of Holiness,
verses.ar: فاذهَبوا إذَن واجعَلوا مِن كُلِّ الأُمَمِ تَلامِيذَ، واغمِسوهُم بِاسمِ الآبِ والابنِ ورُوحِ القُدُسِ،

verses.syr: ܘܐܠܦܘ ܐܢܘܢ ܕܢܛܪܘܢ ܟܠ ܡܐ ܕܦܩܕܬܟܘܢ ܘܗܐ ܐܢܐ ܥܡܟܘܢ ܐܢܐ ܟܠܗܘܢ ܝܘܡܬܐ ܥܕܡܐ ܠܫܘܠܡܗ ܕܥܠܡܐ ܐܡܝܢ
verses.el: διδάσκοντες αὐτοὺς τηρεῖν πάντα ὅσα ἐνετειλάμην ὑμῖν· καὶ ἰδοὺ ἐγὼ μεθ’ ὑμῶν εἰμι πάσας τὰς ἡμέρας ἕως τῆς συντελείας τοῦ αἰῶνος.
verses.en: and teach them to keep all that I have commanded you. And behold, I am with you all days until the end of the age. Amen.
verses.ar: وعَلِّموهُم أن يَحفَظوا كُلَّ ما أوصَيتُكُم بِهِ. وها أنا مَعَكُم كُلَّ الأيّامِ إلى انقِضاءِ الدَّهرِ. آمينَ.

chapters.commentary:
# Annotations on Matthew Chapter 28 — The Rising

## Syriac-First Approach

This chapter is translated primarily from the Syriac Peshitta. The shortest chapter in the passion-resurrection sequence contains the culminating commission: immerse in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Breath of Holiness. The key terms are ܩܡ (qam, rose), ܫܠܛܢܐ (shultana, authority), and ܪܘܚܐ ܕܩܘܕܫܐ (Ruḥa d-Qudsha, Breath of Holiness).

## Root-Recovery Choices

### v.1: ܢܓܗ ܠܚܕ ܒܫܒܐ (nagah l-ḥad b-shaba) — "as it dawned toward the first day of the week"
ܢܓܗ (nagah): to dawn, to grow light. Root ܢ-ܓ-ܗ (n-g-h): shining, light. The resurrection is a dawning — the day that dawns while it is still dark. ܫܒܐ (shaba): the week; ܚܕ ܒܫܒܐ (ḥad b-shaba) = "first of the week" — the day that begins the new cycle. Greek τῇ ἐπιφωσκούσῃ εἰς μίαν σαββάτων (tē epiphōskousē eis mian sabbatōn) = "dawning toward the first of the Sabbath-week."

### v.2: ܙܘܥܐ ܪܒܐ (zuʿa raba) — "great earthquake"
ܙܘܥܐ (zuʿa): earthquake, trembling, shaking. Root ܙ-ܘ-ܥ (z-w-ʿ): to shake, to tremble. The earth had already shaken at the death (27:51); it shakes again at the opening of the tomb. The two seismic events bracket the darkness: death-earthquake and resurrection-earthquake.

### v.3: ܒܪܩܐ (barqa) — "lightning"
Root ܒ-ܪ-ܩ (b-r-q). The angel's appearance: ܐܦܘܗܝ ܐܝܟ ܒܪܩܐ (his face like lightning) and his garment white as snow. The same lightning metaphor used for the parousia (24:27) — "as the lightning goes from east to west, so shall be the coming of the Son of Man." The angel announces what the lightning was already predicting.

### v.6: ܩܡ (qam) — "he has risen"
Root ܩ-ܘ-ܡ (q-w-m): to rise, to stand up, to be established. ܩܡ ܐܝܟ ܡܐ ܕܐܡܪ (qam ayke ma d-amar) = "he has risen just as he said." The simplest, most concrete Syriac word: ܩܡ. Cognate with Hebrew קם (qam, rose/stood) and Arabic قام (qāma, stood/rose). The resurrection is ܩܝܡ (qyam) — and this is the root of ܩܝܡܐ (qyama, covenant/resurrection) in the cup-saying (26:28). "Blood of the ܩܝܡܐ" — the blood of the covenant/rising. The covenant and the rising share their root.

### v.7: ܩܕܡ ܠܟܘܢ ܠܓܠܝܠܐ (qdam l-khun l-Galila) — "he goes before you to the Galil"
ܩܕܡ (qadam): to go before, to precede, to lead. Root ܩ-ܕ-ܡ (q-d-m): front, beforehand. The risen one is already ahead — already preceding his followers into the Galil. The Galil (Galilee) is where it all began (chapter 4); the commission returns to the beginning.

### v.9: ܫܠܡܐ ܠܟܝܢ (shlama l-kheyn) — "Peace to you"
ܫܠܡܐ (shlama): peace, wholeness, completeness. The risen Yeshua's first word to the women: ܫܠܡܐ. Greek χαίρετε (chairete) = "Rejoice!/Greetings!" — a standard Greek greeting. But the Syriac ܫܠܡܐ is the Aramaic greeting — the form of Hebrew שלום (shalom) and the root of Arabic سلام (salām). The risen one greets with the word that means: wholeness has come. The shalom-greeting of the resurrection becomes the Islamic greeting as-salāmu ʿalaykum — peace be upon you.

### v.18: ܫܘܠܛܢܐ (shultana) — "authority"
Root ܫ-ܠ-ܛ (sh-l-ṭ): to rule, to have authority over. ܫܘܠܛܢܐ (shultana) = authority, dominion. "All ܫܘܠܛܢܐ has been given to me in the heavens and on the earth." This is the fulfillment of Daniel 7:14: "to him were given dominion, glory, and sovereignty that all peoples should serve him." Cognate with Arabic سلطان (sulṭān, authority/sultan). The authority that was given to the Son of Man by the Ancient of Days is the authority that now sends the disciples to all nations.

### v.19: ܐܥܡܕܘ ܐܢܘܢ (aʿmedu anun) — "immerse them"
Root ܥ-ܡ-ܕ (ʿ-m-d): to immerse, to go under water, to stand in. NOT "baptize" — the Syriac is the concrete physical action of immersing. This is the same root as Yukhanan's immersion (3:11-16), Yeshua's immersion (3:16), the immersion of suffering (20:22), and the immersion of the commission (28:19). The entire Gospel is framed by immersions: entry into ministry (immersed by Yukhanan) and exit into mission (immerse all nations).

### v.19: ܒܫܡ ܐܒܐ ܘܒܪܐ ܘܪܘܚܐ ܕܩܘܕܫܐ (b-shem Ava w-Bra w-Ruḥa d-Qudsha) — "in the name of the Father and the Son and the Breath of Holiness"
Three terms:
- ܐܒܐ (Ava): Father. Root ܐ-ܒ (ʾ-b). Cognate with Hebrew אב (av) and Arabic أب (ab).
- ܒܪܐ (Bra): Son. Root ܒ-ܪ (b-r): son, child.
- ܪܘܚܐ ܕܩܘܕܫܐ (Ruḥa d-Qudsha): Breath of Holiness. ܪܘܚ (ruaḥ) = breath/wind/spirit. Root ܪ-ܘ-ḥ (r-w-ḥ). Cognate with Hebrew רוח הקדש (ruaḥ ha-qodesh) and Arabic روح القدس (rūḥ al-quds, "Breath/Spirit of Holiness" — the term used for Gabriel in the Qur'an, e.g. 2:87, 2:253). ܩܘܕܫܐ (qudsha): holiness, set-apart-ness. Same root as Hebrew קדוש (qadosh) and Arabic قدس (quds).

The conventional translation "Holy Spirit" obscures: this is the Breath of Holiness — the animating wind that is the breath of the divine holy. The commission is to immerse into the name — the single name — of the three-in-one: Father, Son, Breath-of-Holiness.

### v.20: ܥܕܡܐ ܠܫܘܠܡ ܥܠܡܐ (ʿadma l-shulam ʿalme) — "until the end of the age"
ܫܘܠܡ (shulam): completion, consummation. Root ܫ-ܠ-ܡ (sh-l-m) — the same root as ܫܠܡܐ (peace/greeting, v.9). The Gospel ends where the age ends: "I am with you all days until the ܫܘܠܡ (completion/shalom) of the ܥܠܡܐ (age)." The last word almost: ܫܠܡ — completion, peace, the age fulfilled.

## Cross-Scriptural Root Connections

| Syriac | Hebrew | Arabic | Shared meaning | Qur'an connection |
|--------|--------|--------|----------------|-------------------|
| ܩܡ (qam) | קם (qam) | قام (qāma) | rose/stood | 22:1 — يوم القيامة |
| ܫܠܡ (shlam) | שלום (shalom) | سلام (salām) | peace/wholeness | 6:54 — سلام عليكم |
| ܪܘܚ ܩܘܕܫ (Ruḥ Quds) | רוח הקדש (ruaḥ ha-qodesh) | روح القدس (rūḥ al-quds) | Breath of Holiness | 2:87, 2:253 — روح القدس |
| ܫܘܠܛܢ (shultan) | שלטון (shalṭon) | سلطان (sulṭān) | authority/power | 17:33 |
| ܥܡܕ (ʿamad) | טבל (taval) | — | immerse | — |
| ܩܘܕܫ (qudsha) | קדוש (qadosh) | قدس (quds) | holy/holiness | 59:23 — القدوس |

## Bahá'í Interpretive Notes

The risen Yeshua's commissioning of the disciples — "All authority has been given to me in the heavens and on the earth" — is understood in Bahá'í interpretation as the claim of the Manifestation in his divine station: the authority that belongs to God is invested in the Manifestation for the purposes of the age. The commission to "make disciples of all nations" is the first explicit universalism in Matthew — what began as a mission to "the lost sheep of the house of Israel" (10:6) is now extended to "all the nations." Bahá'u'lláh reads this expansion as the principle of progressive revelation: each Manifestation extends the circle. The immersion formula in the "name" (singular) of Father, Son, and Breath-of-Holiness is read by Bahá'í theologians as pointing to the unity of the divine reality expressed through its Manifestations — three aspects, one name. The closing promise — "I am with you all days until the completion of the age" — is interpreted as the ongoing presence of the divine through successive Manifestations: the Anointed is with the world not in one body but through the continuing appearance of divine Manifestations until the age completes.

## Integrative Links

- **v.6 "he has risen" ↔ Psalm 16:10**: "You will not abandon my breath-life (nafsh) to the Pit, nor let your Holy One see corruption." The resurrection as fulfillment of the Psalm.
- **v.18 all authority given ↔ Daniel 7:14**: "To him were given dominion and glory and sovereignty, that all peoples, nations, and languages should serve him." Matthew ends with the Danielic investiture fulfilled.
- **v.9 Shlama (Peace) ↔ Islamic greeting**: "As-salāmu ʿalaykum" — peace be upon you. The risen Yeshua's greeting in Syriac is word-for-word the Islamic greeting that Muslims exchange. One root, two traditions.
- **v.19 Breath of Holiness ↔ Qur'an 2:87**: وآتينا عيسى ابن مريم البينات وأيدناه بروح القدس — "We gave Jesus son of Mary clear signs and supported him with the Breath/Spirit of Holiness." The same term ܪܘܚܐ ܕܩܘܕܫܐ / روح القدس appears in both the Syriac Gospel commission and the Qur'anic testimony about Jesus.
- **v.19 immerse all nations ↔ Isaiah 2:2-3**: "All nations shall flow to the mountain of the Lord... from Zion shall go forth the Torah." The gathering of all nations to one center — fulfilled in a commission going out from that center.
- **v.20 "I am with you" ↔ Exodus 3:12**: God's promise to Moses: "I will be with you." The same divine assurance given to the one sent is now given by the risen one to those he sends. The circle of commissioning closes.
